review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Test seam strategy relies on runtime-global function variables, creating pervasive low-value indirection.

evidence: `goseg/docker/wireguard.go` declares many aliases like `startContainerForWireguard = StartContainer`, `getLatestContainerInfoForWireguard = GetLatestContainerInfo`, `confForWireguard = config.Conf`.
`goseg/docker/minio.go` and `goseg/routines/docker.go` follow the same pattern (`confForMinIO`, `startContainerForMinIO`, `getContainerStateFn`, `updateWgOnFn`, etc.), and tests mutate those globals.
This pattern makes call paths harder to follow in production and introduces shared mutable seams that couple runtime code structure to test setup.
detail.suggestion: Replace package-global seam variables with explicit dependency structs/interfaces constructed once per module (for example a `DockerRuntime`/`VersionRuntime` with concrete defaults). Keep seam override capability in tests via constructor injection instead of mutable package globals.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Wireguard restart/recovery flow is copy-pasted across handlers and routines instead of abstracted once.

evidence: `handleStartramRestart` in `goseg/handler/startram.go` builds `wgShips`, restarts wireguard, stops ships (`click.BarExit`/`WaitForShipExit`), deletes ship+minio containers, then calls `docker.LoadUrbits`, `docker.LoadMC`, and `docker.LoadMinIOs`.
`Check502Loop` in `goseg/routines/docker.go` repeats the same orchestration sequence when a second 502 strike occurs.
Both implementations encode the same policy but diverge in error aggregation/reporting style, increasing drift risk when restart behavior changes.
detail.suggestion: Extract a single orchestrator (for example `docker.RecoverWireguardFleet(ctx, opts)`) that owns ship discovery, stop/delete/recreate sequencing, and error policy. Call it from both `handleStartramRestart` and `Check502Loop` with scenario-specific options.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Modules depend on full mutable `SysConfig` instead of narrow settings contracts.

evidence: `config.Conf()` returns full `structs.SysConfig` (`goseg/config/config.go`), and call sites across modules pull only subsets (for example `WgRegistered`, `WgOn`, `Piers`, `SnapTime`).
`goseg/main.go`, `goseg/handler/startram.go`, and `goseg/importer/importer.go` each read broad config state for orchestration decisions, coupling unrelated logic to a monolithic bag.
`goseg/config/config.go` already defines narrower snapshots (`StartramSettingsSnapshot`, `ShipSettingsSnapshot`, `RuntimeSettingsSnapshot`) but core callers still use `Conf()` directly.
detail.suggestion: Adopt narrow read models at boundaries: replace direct `Conf()` calls in orchestration paths with dedicated snapshot/accessor APIs per concern (startram, ships, runtime). Phase out `Conf()` usage in non-config packages except where full state is genuinely required.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Container bootstrap implementations repeat the same skeleton without a shared abstraction.

evidence: `LoadWireguard` and `LoadNetdata` both implement: check/open config file, create default if missing, write generated config, start container, update container state.
`LoadMC` in `goseg/docker/minio.go` applies the same startup skeleton with minor policy differences (registration gate and names).
The repeated skeleton causes policy and error-handling behavior to be scattered across multiple loaders rather than represented as one reusable boot contract.
detail.suggestion: Create a shared loader pipeline abstraction (for example `bootService(spec)` with hooks for preconditions, default-file creation, config materialization, container start, and state update) and migrate wireguard/netdata/mc loaders to it.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Shipcreator’s config interface is a mostly pass-through wrapper with one concrete implementation.

evidence: `ConfigService` in `goseg/shipcreator/shipcreator.go` is implemented only by `configService`, whose methods directly forward to `config` package functions (`Conf`, `UrbitConf`, `UrbitConfAll`, `UpdateUrbitConfig`, `UpdateConfTyped`).
This interface adds call indirection but little policy/translation logic; the abstraction boundary does not represent a distinct domain model.
A package-global `defaultService` further hides construction and keeps static coupling to global config behavior.
detail.suggestion: Either remove the pass-through interface and call `config` directly, or strengthen the abstraction by introducing a true repository/service contract with focused methods that encode ship-creation invariants (ports, persistence, pier list mutation) instead of forwarding raw config operations.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::uplo.summary: Upload-path testing is documented as required but not encoded as an enforceable quality gate.

evidence: README.md states upload-path changes should include at least one deterministic unit test.
README.md quality gates are command-based guidance and do not define a CI-enforced check for upload-specific coverage/contract tests.
goseg/handler/upload.go is a high-risk dispatch surface with multiple behavior branches, so relying on manual adherence creates regression risk.
detail.suggestion: Add a mandatory CI check that fails when upload-path files change without corresponding targeted tests (for example via path-aware test selection, changed-files policy, or coverage threshold scoped to handler/upload flows), and document that rule in README.md.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::erro.summary: Security-relevant redaction behavior lacks explicit test strategy coverage in project contracts.

evidence: goseg/startram/errors.go constructs externally visible masked errors while preserving internal cause chains via `Unwrap()`.
README.md backend quality gate does not specify redaction/PII-safety assertions or error-contract tests for masking functions.
A regression in message masking or unwrap behavior would not be specifically caught by the documented gate.
detail.suggestion: Extend the backend quality gate documentation to require explicit tests for error redaction contracts (masked external message, preserved internal cause), and add package-level tests covering leak-prevention edge cases.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::bran.summary: Action-routing logic has no documented branch test matrix, increasing fragility when actions evolve.

evidence: goseg/handler/upload.go routes behavior by string actions and has three outcome classes: `open-endpoint`, `reset`, and default error.
README.md requires generic tests for upload paths but does not define branch-level expectations (unknown action handling, payload decode failure, service error propagation).
Without a required matrix, future action additions can silently omit negative-path assertions.
detail.suggestion: Define and enforce a minimal branch-matrix test checklist for action handlers (decode failure, each supported action success/failure, unsupported action), and tie it to PR/CI policy for handler action surfaces.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::s.summary: New-ship and import flows duplicate the same asynchronous lifecycle orchestration, causing parallel logic drift risk.

evidence: `waitForShipReady` in `newship.go` (lines 173-226) and `importer.go` (lines 577-643) both poll `click.GetLusCode`, conditionally switch to wireguard, delete/restart containers, and finalize transitions.
`newShipToggleRemote` (lines 228-246) and `importShipToggleRemote` (lines 645-663) are effectively the same loop with different transition publishers.
`newShipRegisterService` (lines 284-288) and `registerServices` (lines 786-790) duplicate the same Startram registration call.
detail.suggestion: Extract a shared `ShipProvisioningOrchestrator` (phase runner + transition adapter) used by both new-ship and import flows, with pluggable transition emitters and optional phases (e.g., extraction/fix-acme).

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::s.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::u.summary: Urbit maintenance operations reimplement the same multi-step container lifecycle instead of using a common operation primitive.

evidence: `ChopPier` (471-540), `rollChopPier` (980-1063), and `packMeldPier` (295-380+) all perform status check, conditional stop with `|exit`, `WaitComplete`, boot-status mutation, restart, and transition publish cycles.
These functions duplicate error-wrapper closures and delayed transition cleanup (`time.Sleep` + publish empty event), which increases divergence surface for behavior fixes.
`WaitComplete` exists as shared polling in `urbit.go` (147-190), but higher-level maintenance choreography remains copy-pasted.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a reusable maintenance state-machine helper that accepts ordered phases (`stop`, `run bootStatus`, `wait`, `restore`) and transition metadata, then have chop/roll/pack-meld compose phase lists.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::u.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::b.summary: Broadcast state construction is concentrated in one large function that mixes unrelated responsibilities.

evidence: `ConstructPierInfo` (150-394) performs local backup filesystem scanning, remote backup flattening, docker status/stats retrieval, click desk probing, Startram subdomain readiness checks, and UI struct assembly in one routine.
The function reads from config, docker, click, filesystem, and cached broadcast state in one pass, making behavior changes cross-cutting and hard to test in isolation.
The same function contains domain normalization logic (pack day/date defaults, alias visibility, backup projections), not just data collection.
detail.suggestion: Split into composable providers: `backupSnapshot`, `runtimeSnapshot`, `startramSnapshot`, and `urbitViewAssembler`; keep `ConstructPierInfo` as thin coordination only.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: Domain handlers directly embed websocket/UI transition protocol details, coupling business orchestration to transport semantics.

evidence: Multiple handlers inline repeated event lifecycle patterns (`Publish... loading/error/success`, `time.Sleep`, clear) instead of a shared transition transaction helper.

`startram.go` repeats local `handleError` closures in separate actions (`handleStartramRegister`, `handleStartramEndpoint`, `handleStartramCancel`) with slightly different event types and reset behavior.
`support.go` and `newship.go` also encode transition timing and cleanup sequencing directly in business flow, making orchestration logic depend on UI event choreography.
detail.suggestion: Create a shared transition-emitter utility (start/progress/fail/succeed/clear with policy-driven delays) and keep handlers focused on domain steps + typed outcomes.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::c.summary: Configuration package combines initialization, migration, key management, runtime state, and utility concerns in one central module.

evidence: `config.go` is 1205 lines and `init()` (60-173) performs filesystem creation, key setup, merge/migration, hash computation, persistence, and starts a goroutine (`ConfChannel`).
The package also stores mutable runtime registries (`GSContainers`, `startramConfig`, minio password map) alongside disk persistence and random/hash helpers.
Sibling files (`urbit.go`, `version.go`, `mc.go`) use better-scoped persistence helpers, but `config.go` remains a catch-all orchestration center.
detail.suggestion: Split `config` into explicit subpackages/components: `bootstrap`, `store` (disk persistence), `runtime_state`, and `credentials`; make initialization explicit from `main` instead of heavy `init()` side effects.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::c.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::w.summary: WiFi module mixes OS command orchestration, AP lifecycle, websocket request handling, and periodic broadcast loops in one package surface.

evidence: `C2CMode`, `C2CConnect`, and `UnaliveC2C` orchestrate system services and shell commands, while `CaptiveAPI` handles websocket protocol parsing and `announceNetworks` manages long-lived client broadcasts in the same file.
`C2CConnect` includes system reboot side effects (`execCommandForWiFi("reboot")`), while `CaptiveAPI` may also restart groundseg, indicating competing lifecycle paths in transport-facing code.
Shared mutable `clients` map is managed in the same module that executes networking shell commands, increasing responsibility spread.
detail.suggestion: Separate WiFi domain service (connect/AP state transitions) from captive websocket transport handlers and from network-announcement publisher; expose a narrow service API consumed by handlers.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::w.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

subjective_review::goseg/handler/wiregua.summary: No design review on record — run `desloppify review --prepare`

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Cross-module boundary policies are implemented ad hoc per package, creating inconsistent coupling patterns and hidden integration costs.

evidence: upload.go uses a mutable package-level service locator (`defaultUploadService` + `SetUploadService`) to coordinate dependencies.
errors.go defines a custom package-local wrapping/redaction boundary (`maskedWrappedError`) for external API failures.
Both files implement edge-boundary behavior locally with different mechanisms, indicating no shared architectural contract for dependency injection and boundary error semantics.
detail.suggestion: Define a shared boundary pattern for edge modules: constructor-injected dependencies (no package-global mutation) plus a common boundary error contract/utilities package for redaction and wrapping. Migrate handler/startram edges to that contract and remove ad hoc local patterns.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Upload handler relies on mutable global service state, creating order-dependent behavior across modules.

evidence: upload.go declares `var defaultUploadService UploadService = importerUploadService{}` and allows runtime replacement via `SetUploadService`.
Handler execution (`UploadHandler`) reads this global directly, so behavior depends on who last mutated shared package state.
Compared with startram/errors.go's pure function-style wrapper (`wrapAPIConnectionError(err)`), this mutable approach introduces broader cross-module side-effect risk.
detail.suggestion: Replace `SetUploadService`/global state with explicit dependency injection (e.g., a handler struct receiving `UploadService`), and pass that dependency from composition root to eliminate shared mutable coordination.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Shipcreator’s `ConfigService` abstraction is mostly pass-through and does not create a real boundary.

evidence: `configService` methods in `shipcreator.go` are direct one-line forwards (`Conf`, `UrbitConf`, `UrbitConfAll`, `UpdateUrbitConfig`, `UpdatePiers`) to global `config` package functions.
Top-level shipcreator functions use `defaultService = NewService(nil)`, so callers still depend on a global concrete implementation instead of dependency-selected boundaries.
`importer/importer.go` calls package-level shipcreator functions, which bypass explicit dependency wiring at call sites.
detail.suggestion: Either remove `ConfigService` and call `config` directly (reduce indirection), or make the boundary honest: inject `Service` explicitly where used (e.g., importer/handlers), eliminate global `defaultService`, and define a narrower interface around only operations needed by each caller.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Repeated `public wrapper -> withRuntime(newRuntime())` pattern inflates indirection and API surface without proportional leverage.

evidence: In `wireguard.go`, many functions are duplicated (`LoadWireguard/loadWireguard`, `wgContainerConf/wgContainerConfWithRuntime`, `buildWgConf/buildWgConfWithRuntime`, `WriteWgConf/writeWgConfWithRuntime`, file-copy helpers).
Same pattern appears in `netdata.go`, `minio.go`, `routines/docker.go`, and `routines/version.go`, creating two call paths per behavior.

Runtime structs include function fields that point to local wrappers (`writeWgConf: writeWgConfWithRuntime`, `writeNDConf: writeNDConfWithRuntime`), increasing conceptual load and making ownership of side effects harder to track.
detail.suggestion: Consolidate behind one explicit runtime boundary per subsystem: keep internal runtime-injected methods for tests, but expose one production entrypoint per operation and collapse duplicate wrapper layers. Consider constructor-injected service structs over free-function pairs.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Wide access to full `SysConfig` creates a leaky, low-cohesion dependency surface across unrelated modules.

evidence: `config.Conf()` returns full `structs.SysConfig`, and many modules call it directly for small subsets (sessions in `auth`, profile/app/system fields in `broadcast`, update settings in `routines/version`, endpoint/piers in handlers).
Even where snapshot helpers exist (`StartramSettingsSnapshot`, `SwapSettingsSnapshot`, etc.), modules still frequently consume broad config state directly.
This makes module boundaries implicit and brittle: changing `SysConfig` shape or initialization behavior can ripple across multiple subsystems.
detail.suggestion: Move callers to narrower typed snapshots/capabilities (e.g., auth settings, startram settings, update settings) and reserve full `Conf()` access for config package internals. Introduce focused read APIs per domain and phase out direct `SysConfig` reads in handlers/routines.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Operation lifecycle orchestration is reimplemented in multiple modules instead of using a shared abstraction.

evidence: `handler/urbit_operations.go` repeats similar transition scaffolding across many operations (publish loading/error/success, polling loops, post-operation clears).
`handler/startram.go` uses separate ad-hoc orchestration/error collection logic (`appendOrchestrationError`, repeated event publish/sleep/reset flows).
`importer/importer.go` and `rectify/rectify.go` each carry their own transition/error publication patterns, indicating missing reusable operation lifecycle abstraction.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared operation runner abstraction for long-running workflows (status transitions, error wrapping, retry/poll cadence, completion cleanup) and compose operation-specific steps into it. This reduces duplicated orchestration code and clarifies business logic vs lifecycle plumbing.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Docker boundary setup/teardown is repeatedly inlined instead of abstracted into a consistent execution primitive.

evidence: `docker/docker.go` repeatedly creates/closes clients in many functions (`GetShipStatus`, `GetContainerRunningStatus`, `GetContainerNetwork`, `CreateVolume`, `DeleteVolume`, etc.).
`handler/support.go` and `importer/importer.go` instantiate separate docker clients directly for workflow steps.
This duplicates transport-level concerns and encourages subtle inconsistency in timeout/context/cleanup policies between call sites.
detail.suggestion: Add a small shared Docker execution helper/service (e.g., `WithClient(ctx, fn)` or injected client provider) that centralizes client creation, timeout policy, and cleanup; migrate repeated call sites to that boundary.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Background routines depend on handler package, reversing expected directory layering

evidence: goseg/routines/chop.go imports groundseg/handler solely to call handler.ChopPier from periodic maintenance flow.
Assigned handler files are request/WS entry logic (DevHandler, Login/NewShip/C2C handlers), so routines->handler coupling ties scheduler code to transport directory ownership.

Stage 2: update imports in `goseg/routines/chop.go` and handler callers to the new package. Stage 3: validate with `go test ./goseg/routines ./goseg/handler` then `go test ./...`, and verify no residual handler dependency from routines via `rg "groundseg/handler" goseg/routines`.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Backup storage path ownership is duplicated across handler and routines packages

evidence: goseg/handler/dev.go defines `BackupDir` using `system.IsMountedMMC(config.BasePath)` and fallback `filepath.Join(config.BasePath, "backup")`.
goseg/routines/backup.go defines another `BackupDir` using equivalent logic via `setBackupDir()`, creating split ownership of backup filesystem placement policy.
detail.suggestion: Stage 1: introduce a single target folder for backup infrastructure policy (for example `goseg/backups/path/` or extend `goseg/backups/`) with one exported resolver function. Stage 2: move both `BackupDir` initializations to consume that resolver in `goseg/handler/dev.go` and `goseg/routines/backup.go`. Stage 3: run import updates and validation with `go test ./goseg/handler ./goseg/routines ./goseg/backups` then `go test ./...`; confirm only one path policy definition remains using `rg "IsMountedMMC\(config.BasePath\)|BackupDir =" goseg`.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: click directory is overloaded with many domain commands and command infrastructure in one flat package

evidence: holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles reports goseg/click/ has 25 files (13 production, 12 tests), which exceeds the mixed-concern threshold for flat packages.
Assigned files show domain-specific command wrappers (`fixAcme`, `backupAgent`) co-located with shared execution/filter seams instead of separated by domain vs runtime infrastructure.
detail.suggestion: Stage 1: create target subfolders under click, e.g. `goseg/click/commands/acme`, `goseg/click/commands/backup`, and `goseg/click/runtime` for shared exec/filter/hoon helpers. Stage 2: move domain files first (`acme.go`, `backup.go`, matching tests), then move shared helpers and update package imports. review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Upload handler is tightly coupled to importer command shape, making transport and importer boundaries change together.

evidence: UploadHandler unmarshals websocket payload and directly constructs importer.OpenUploadEndpointCmd field-by-field before invoking defaultUploadService.OpenUploadEndpoint.
importerUploadService is a pass-through adapter that forwards directly to importer package functions, so handler layer owns importer contract details instead of a boundary service.
README runtime-boundary guidance expects stable handler/service boundaries, but command translation is embedded at the handler edge.
detail.suggestion: Introduce an upload application service boundary (`goseg/uploadsvc` or equivalent) that owns command validation/translation; make handler depend on a service DTO independent of importer.OpenUploadEndpointCmd; wire importer adapter only at composition root so importer API changes do not force websocket-handler rewrites.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::glob.summary: Global mutable upload service dependency creates test-order fragility for contract-critical upload routing.

evidence: defaultUploadService is a package-global variable and SetUploadService mutates it process-wide.
No production-side scoping helper exists to restore prior service after overrides, increasing risk of leaking test doubles across tests.
README requires deterministic upload-path testing and explicit branch-matrix coverage; global mutable dependencies undermine deterministic isolation if not strictly reset.
detail.suggestion: Replace global mutation with instance-based dependency injection (e.g., `type UploadHandlerDeps struct { Service UploadService }`) and construct handler with explicit deps in tests; if short-term retention is needed, add `SwapUploadServiceForTest(t *testing.T, svc UploadService)` with automatic `t.Cleanup` restore and enforce its use in upload tests.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Masked external error contract is weakly enforced because outward message formatting still embeds raw error text before narrow masking.

evidence: wrapAPIConnectionError formats outward message with `%v` from underlying error and then applies only maskPubkey.
If upstream errors include sensitive non-pubkey tokens/URLs, current masking policy can leak details despite preserved cause-chain intent.
README contract requires redacted outward message with preserved internal cause chain, but runtime implementation encodes only a partial redaction policy.
detail.suggestion: Move to a structured error policy: expose a constant outward message (no raw `%v`), keep full cause in `cause` only, and add explicit classifier-based redaction where needed; codify contract tests to assert forbidden substrings from representative upstream errors while keeping `errors.Is/Unwrap` behavior.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Governance and quality-contract material is co-located with install/uninstall content in root README, reducing contract discoverability and ownership clarity.

evidence: README contains operational install/uninstall sections plus architecture boundaries, test policy, and CI contract gate details in one file.
The same README defines concrete code-level obligations for upload.go and errors.go, but those contracts are not housed near backend docs hierarchy.
holistic_context.structure shows large backend directories (handler 26 files, startram 15 files), increasing need for precise, local contract documentation.
detail.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 create `docs/governance/` and `docs/testing/`; Stage 2 move backend contract sections into `docs/governance/backend-contracts.md` and upload/startram expectations into `docs/testing/backend-quality-gates.md`; Stage 3 leave concise pointers in README and remove duplicated policy text; Stage 4 update internal links and references with `rg -n "Backend Quality Gate|Upload Contract Gate|masked external errors|upload.go" README.md docs`; Stage 5 validate with `go test ./goseg/handler ./goseg/startram` and run existing CI script `.github/scripts/check-upload-contract.sh` to confirm docs and gates still align.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.summary: Ship create/import flows duplicate the same lifecycle orchestration instead of sharing one workflow.

evidence: Both `waitForShipReady` implementations poll `click.GetLusCode` on 1s tick, then optionally toggle remote networking and restart containers (`newship.go` and `importer.go`).
Both files implement separate `*ToggleRemote` loops with the same startram subdomain status polling pattern.
Both files implement independent cleanup routines (`errorCleanup`) that remove urbit config, system entry, and volume with similar behavior but different transition semantics.
`NewShipHandler` also contains drive-formatting prechecks that overlap with upload session drive handling in importer, spreading one workflow across multiple handlers.
detail.suggestion: Extract a shared ship lifecycle service (e.g., `shipworkflow`) with reusable steps for drive resolution/formatting, boot wait, remote-network switch, cleanup, and service registration; keep handlers thin and pass context-specific transition emitters.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::u.summary: Urbit operations file is a god module with repeated state-transition scaffolding and mixed responsibilities.

evidence: `urbit_operations.go` contains many heterogeneous commands (maintenance, domain management, export, lifecycle toggles) behind one action map in `urbit.go`, with no cohesive sub-boundaries.

Multiple operations repeat the same transition lifecycle pattern (`loading`/`error`/`success`/reset) and similar stop-restart container choreography (`togglePower`, `toggleNetwork`, `rebuildContainer`, `handleLoom`, `handleSnapTime`, `rollChopPier`, `ChopPier`, `packMeldPier`).
The module directly coordinates config persistence, docker operations, click commands, and broadcast side effects in each function, making behavior changes require edits across many functions.
detail.suggestion: Split urbit commands by concern (e.g., `maintenance`, `network`, `domain`, `backup/export`) and introduce shared helpers for transition emission and stop/restart sequences to remove repeated orchestration code.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::u.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::h.summary: Handlers are tightly coupled to websocket protocol details and call each other via payload re-marshalling.

evidence: `NewShipHandler` handles domain flow but, for cancel, constructs a websocket payload, marshals it, and re-enters `UrbitHandler` instead of invoking a shared domain operation directly.
`handleStartramToggle` builds `WsUrbitPayload`, marshals JSON, and dispatches to `UrbitHandler` inside orchestration loops, coupling startram behavior to urbit transport contracts.
Action handlers publish UI event strings inline while also mutating system state, so protocol changes and business behavior changes are entangled.
detail.suggestion: Introduce handler-independent application services (e.g., `ShipService`) and make handlers only parse/validate payloads and call typed service methods; centralize websocket event mapping in a separate adapter layer.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::h.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::w.summary: WiFi subsystem combines unrelated concerns in one module with broad mutable global state.

evidence: `wifi.go` owns websocket client map and message loop (`CaptiveAPI`), captive portal lifecycle, AP mode toggling, command execution (`nmcli`, `systemctl`, `ip`, `reboot`), and periodic discovery loops in one file.
Global mutable package state (`clients`, `WifiInfo`, `Device`, `ConfChannel`, `c2cEnabled`) is updated by long-running loops and request handlers, increasing hidden coupling and lifecycle complexity.
`handler.C2CHandler` delegates directly into this mixed module, so UI-triggered connect flow is tightly bound to OS/network orchestration details.
detail.suggestion: Separate WiFi concerns into focused components: command runner/device discovery, AP/captive control, and websocket transport adapter; expose a small service interface for C2C actions and state updates.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::w.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Several mutating workflows swallow operation failures and still return success, leaving partial state changes unreported.

evidence: `urbit_operations.go` logs service/config/broadcast update failures during delete/export/rebuild flows and still returns `nil` in success path (lines 578-597, 619-622, 720-727).
`newship.go` logs failures while removing config/volume and does not propagate cleanup failures (lines 264-274); `os.RemoveAll` is called without checking error (line 279).
`backup.go` logs failed remote uploads and continues loop without surfacing scheduler failure (lines 81-84).
detail.suggestion: Define a shared policy for mutating operations: classify errors as recoverable vs terminal, accumulate recoverable failures, and return a non-nil error (or explicit partial-failure result) whenever required side effects fail.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Production code frequently discards I/O/runtime errors with blank identifiers, creating silent failure paths.

evidence: `system.go` ignores errors from `ReadDir`, `Readlink`, and mountpoint decoding (lines 62, 65, 86).
`wireguard.go` ignores `ReadAll` failure before JSON decode (line 34).
`config.go` ignores `os.Open` and `json.Marshal` errors (lines 81, 167, 886).
`importer.go` ignores `filepath.Walk` result and several `MkdirAll`/`ReadDir` errors during filesystem restructuring (lines 728, 754-755, 765).
`minio.go` ignores container status polling errors (line 247).
detail.suggestion: Ban `_` for `error` in production paths via linting, then replace each ignored error with explicit handling: return wrapped error for required operations, or log-and-fallback with a clear fallback branch and telemetry.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Mixed `%w` and `%v` wrapping semantics break consistent error-chain inspection across modules.

evidence: `urbit.go` preserves causes with `%w` (line 156), but nearby urbit operation handlers format with `%v` (e.g., `urbit_operations.go` line 33).
`config/urbit.go` returns stringified `%v` errors for load/decode/persist operations (lines 113, 117, 140, 167).
`system/disk.go` and `click/acme.go` also stringify upstream failures with `%v` (disk lines 44, 48; acme lines 30, 37, 41).
detail.suggestion: Adopt a project-wide wrapping rule: use `%w` for propagated errors and `%v` only in terminal log messages. Add helper constructors for common operation contexts to keep messages consistent while preserving causes.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Environment probe failures are silently ignored in backup path selection, masking storage-detection problems.

evidence: Each module calls `system.IsMountedMMC(config.BasePath)` and discards the error (`handler/dev.go` line 24, `broadcast/broadcast.go` line 691, `routines/backup.go` line 203).
On probe failure, logic silently defaults to local backup path, indistinguishable from a valid non-MMC host.
detail.suggestion: Return `(path, error)` from backup-dir resolvers, log probe failure with context, and make fallback explicit (including a metric or warning state) so operators can distinguish true non-MMC from detection failure.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

boilerplate_duplication::goseg/docker/ne.summary: Boilerplate block repeated across 2 files (window 15 lines): goseg/docker/netdata.go:130, goseg/docker/wireguard.go:141

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Handler package hard-wires service construction through a global singleton

evidence: `defaultUploadHandler` is created at package scope in `goseg/handler/upload.go`, and `NewUploadMessageHandler(nil)` conditionally constructs `uploadsvc.NewImporterService()`.
The exported entrypoint `UploadHandler` cannot be called with an alternate implementation, so module boundaries are fixed by import-time/global state rather than explicit composition.
By contrast, `goseg/startram/errors.go` is a pure helper with explicit wrapping behavior, highlighting inconsistent boundary design between sibling subsystems.
detail.suggestion: Remove package-global fallback wiring from `goseg/handler/upload.go`. Require `uploadsvc.Service` injection from app/bootstrap composition, expose an instance method handler as the primary entrypoint, and keep optional global adapters in a dedicated wiring layer (not inside handler package business boundary).

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Cross-module boundary policy is inconsistent for error shaping and exposure

evidence: `goseg/startram/errors.go` centralizes API-facing masking via `errpolicy.WrapMasked(apiConnectionErrorMessage, err)`.
`goseg/handler/upload.go` emits boundary errors inline (`fmt.Errorf(...)`) with mixed message styles and no shared policy abstraction.
Different boundary modules applying different error-shaping mechanisms indicates architectural drift in cross-module contracts.
detail.suggestion: Define a shared boundary error contract for handler-like modules (for example, a small package-level adapter that standardizes masking/wrapping strategy) and migrate both subsystems to that contract so boundary behavior is consistent and maintainable.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: The codebase defines narrow config snapshots but frequently bypasses them with direct global config reads, weakening abstraction boundaries.

evidence: `goseg/config/config.go` introduces typed snapshot abstractions (`StartramSettingsSnapshot`, `ShipSettingsSnapshot`, `SwapSettingsSnapshot`).

`goseg/broadcast/broadcast.go` mixes snapshots with direct globals (`config.GetStartramConfig()`, `config.LoadUrbitConfig()`, `config.UrbitConf()`, `config.Conf()`), so consumers can bypass the narrowed contracts.

`goseg/handler/startram.go` similarly uses snapshots for some values but directly reads `config.UrbitConf()` and `config.GetStartramConfig()` in orchestration paths, so no single stable abstraction is enforced.
detail.suggestion: Define a single read-model interface for runtime handlers/broadcast (for example, `ConfigView` with explicit typed getters) and migrate callers to that API; deprecate direct `Conf/GetStartramConfig/UrbitConf` reads outside `config` internals.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Large runtime structs are used as function bags for tests, adding indirection cost without a cohesive domain abstraction.

evidence: `versionRuntime` in `goseg/routines/version.go` aggregates many unrelated function fields; `testVersionRuntime` in `goseg/routines/version_test.go` must stub almost all of them even for focused tests.

`dockerRoutineRuntime`, `urbitRuntime`, and `wireguardRuntime` repeat the same pattern across modules with one production constructor each, creating wrapper chains around package-level functions rather than explicit service boundaries.
Most fields are direct pass-throughs to package functions (`config.*`, `docker.*`, `handler.*`, `click.*`) and do not enforce policy/translation at the abstraction seam.
detail.suggestion: Replace broad function-bag runtimes with smaller role-specific interfaces (e.g., version metadata source, container lifecycle service, urbit config store) and inject only the dependencies each workflow actually uses.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Using `any` in `UrbitDocker` leaks serialization concerns into business logic and forces repeated runtime type assertions.

evidence: `goseg/structs/configs.go` defines operational fields as `any` (`CustomPierLocation`, `StartramReminder`, `ChopOnUpgrade`, `DisableShipRestarts`) and compensates with custom coercion in `UnmarshalJSON`.
`goseg/routines/docker.go` must guard with `if disableRestart, ok := conf.DisableShipRestarts.(bool); ok ...`, showing callers cannot trust the type contract.
`goseg/handler/urbit_operations.go` repeats assertions such as `customLoc, ok := shipConf.CustomPierLocation.(string)`, pushing schema uncertainty into handlers.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a strongly typed config model for runtime use (bool/string/pointer fields), keep legacy coercion isolated at load boundaries, and remove downstream type assertions from handlers/routines.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: `shipcreator` uses a pass-through interface/implementation pair that adds indirection but no policy or adaptation.

evidence: `ConfigService` methods in `goseg/shipcreator/shipcreator.go` map 1:1 to existing `config` package functions, and `configService` is only a forwarding adapter.
The abstraction does not translate models, validate invariants, or hide backend volatility; it mostly duplicates existing API shape and increases call surface.
A package-level `defaultService` further couples this layer to global state instead of introducing a meaningful boundary.
detail.suggestion: Either collapse the pass-through interface and call `config` directly, or promote it into a real domain boundary by moving invariant checks, transaction semantics, and persistence orchestration behind the service.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Background routine package depends on handler package, inverting layer boundaries.

evidence: goseg/routines/chop.go imports groundseg/handler and directly wires chopPierForChop = handler.ChopPier.
Handler files in this batch are websocket action handlers (DevHandler/NewShip/Login/etc.), so routines currently reaches into an action-handling package for core operational work.
detail.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create a domain service package such as goseg/ships or goseg/operations/chop and move ChopPier implementation there; (2) update goseg/routines/chop.go to depend on the new service package instead of groundseg/handler; (3) keep handler package as an adapter that calls the same service for websocket-triggered chop actions. Move order: extract service -> update routines imports -> update handler call sites -> remove handler-owned chop implementation. Import-update/validation commands: `rg -n "handler\.ChopPier|ChopPier\(" goseg`, `gofmt -w goseg/handler goseg/routines goseg/ships`, `go test ./goseg/...`.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Backup orchestration is duplicated across handler and routines packages instead of one owner.

evidence: Both goseg/handler/dev.go and goseg/routines/backup.go define a package-level BackupDir computed from IsMountedMMC/config.BasePath and perform backup directory orchestration.
Handler dev actions perform backup creation/upload logic that substantially overlaps with routines backup flows, creating multiple package entry points for the same operational concern.
detail.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create a single backup domain package (for example goseg/backupsvc) to own backup path resolution, local backup creation, and remote upload selection; (2) move shared BackupDir/path helpers first, then move backup execution logic from handler/dev and routines/backup into backupsvc; (3) leave handler/dev.go and routines/backup.go as thin adapters that call backupsvc APIs. Move order: shared path helper -> local backup flow -> remote backup flow -> adapter cleanup. Import-update/validation commands: `rg -n "BackupDir|CreateBackup|UploadBackup|remote-backup-tlon|TlonBackup" goseg`, `gofmt -w goseg/handler goseg/routines goseg/backupsvc`, `go test ./goseg/...`.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: The click package is a large flat namespace mixing unrelated command domains.

evidence: holistic_context.structure reports goseg/click/ with 25 files, while assigned files show unrelated concerns (acme certificate reset and backup agent state extraction) living in the same package.
Domain-specific click recipes are grouped by transport package name (`click`) rather than domain boundary, which obscures ownership and increases cross-module navigation cost.
detail.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) introduce subpackages under click by domain, e.g. goseg/click/acme and goseg/click/backup (or domain-first packages that use click helpers); (2) move recipe functions first while preserving shared execute/filter helpers in a small internal helper file/package; (3) update callers in handler/routines to import the new domain package paths. Move order: create subpackages -> move acme recipes -> move backup recipes -> update imports and delete old shims. Import-update/validation commands: `rg -n "fixAcme|backupAgent|groundseg/click" goseg`, `gofmt -w goseg/click goseg/handler goseg/routines`, `go test ./goseg/...`.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Upload handler runtime boundaries drift from documented DI and translation contracts.

evidence: README states handlers should be dependency-injected without package-global service mutation and that transport-to-domain translation should live in `goseg/uploadsvc`.
`goseg/handler/upload.go` defines `defaultUploadHandler` as a package-global singleton and performs request field translation inside the websocket handler.
detail.suggestion: Refactor upload flow so websocket entrypoints receive `uploadsvc.Service` through explicit constructor wiring at startup, remove package-global `defaultUploadHandler`, and move request translation into an `uploadsvc` adapter (`FromWsUploadPayload`) so handler code only validates/dispatches actions.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::cont.summary: Critical governance contracts are prose-first and not co-located as executable checks.

evidence: README requires explicit upload branch-matrix coverage and masked-error chain assertions, but the reviewed runtime files do not encode these contracts in assertable structures (e.g., branch tables/contract constants).
`upload.go` contains multiple string-routed behavior branches and wrapped errors where compatibility expectations are easy to drift without targeted tests.
`errors.go` wraps outward messages while preserving causes, a behavior that requires regression tests to remain stable.
detail.suggestion: Create co-located contract tests and fixtures: (1) table-driven action matrix tests for `UploadMessageHandler.Handle`, (2) masking contract tests for `wrapAPIConnectionError` asserting outward message redaction and `errors.Is` preservation, and (3) keep CI gate path checks but derive required action cases from a shared test table to prevent branch drift.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Large flattened handler package obscures transport/domain ownership boundaries.

evidence: holistic structure data shows `goseg/handler/` has 26 files (mixed endpoints and orchestration), matching flat-directory overload signals.
`upload.go` contains both transport routing and request translation logic while README defines a narrower handler ownership boundary.
detail.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 target folders: create `goseg/handler/ws/` for websocket transport handlers and `goseg/uploadsvc/adapters/` for payload-to-command translation. Stage 2 move order: move upload transport entry (`upload.go`) to `handler/ws/upload.go`, then extract mapping code to `uploadsvc/adapters/upload_payload.go`, then update call sites/imports. Stage 3 import-update/validation commands: run `gofmt -w goseg/handler/ws goseg/uploadsvc/adapters goseg/handler`, then `cd goseg && go test ./...`, then `go test -tags=integration ./broadcast ./handler ./routines ./ws` in integration-capable environments.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.summary: New-ship and import flows duplicate the same multi-step orchestration logic instead of sharing a single lifecycle workflow.

evidence: `createUrbitShip` and `configureUploadedPier` both perform config creation, system config mutation, container/volume prep, startup, optional remote/network switching, and readiness polling.
`waitForShipReady` in both files uses near-identical ticker loops around `click.GetLusCode`, then conditionally toggles to wireguard mode and restarts containers.
Both modules also carry parallel cleanup routines (`errorCleanup`) with overlapping side effects.
detail.suggestion: Extract a shared ship-provisioning pipeline package with reusable steps (prepare-storage, bootstrap-config, start, wait-ready, optional-remote-switch, cleanup), and make new-ship/import pass strategy-specific options.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::t.summary: UI transition state handling is scattered and duplicated across handlers, producing inconsistent orchestration structure.

evidence: Multiple handlers manually implement similar patterns: publish loading state, run operation, publish success/error, sleep, then clear event.
Examples include `handleStartramRegister`, `handleStartramEndpoint`, `setUrbitDomain`, `setMinIODomain`, `rebuildContainer`, and `SupportHandler`.
Error handling is repeatedly encoded as local closures that also mutate transition channels.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared transition runner/helper that standardizes begin/success/error/clear behavior with configurable labels and timeout behavior, then migrate handler actions to it.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::t.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::s.summary: Support-report handling combines too many concerns in one module, reducing structural clarity and change isolation.

evidence: `SupportHandler` coordinates payload parsing, transition state, on-disk report assembly, optional profiling, zip generation, cleanup, and HTTP submission.
`dumpBugReport` directly couples report format decisions with Docker/system config/log data extraction and sanitization logic.
The file also owns transport concerns (`sendBugReport`) and filesystem/archive concerns (`zipDir`, `copyFile`, `sanitizeJSON`).
detail.suggestion: Split support flow into focused components (collector, sanitizer, archiver, uploader, transition notifier) behind a small orchestration layer in the handler package.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::s.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::c.summary: Global config initialization centralizes unrelated startup work in `init()`, creating a high-coupling bootstrap path.

evidence: `config.init()` handles default file creation, key generation, merge/migration, hash calculation, persistence round-trip, and starts `ConfChannel` goroutine.
This startup path mutates global singleton state (`globalConfig`, `BasePath`, key files) while also performing disk I/O and long-lived goroutine setup.
Adjacent config files use narrower responsibilities, but startup remains centralized in one broad initializer.
detail.suggestion: Replace implicit `init()` orchestration with explicit startup phases (`load`, `migrate`, `ensure-secrets`, `publish-state`, `start-watchers`) invoked from a top-level bootstrap function.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::c.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::w.summary: WiFi subsystem blends transport API, device control, and runtime state management in one package-level module.

evidence: `wifi.go` contains websocket API endpoint handling (`CaptiveAPI`), captive portal startup, shell-command orchestration, and mutable singleton state.
Global variables (`clients`, `WifiInfo`, `Device`, `ConfChannel`, `c2cEnabled`) couple unrelated concerns and make behavior stateful across calls.
Operational flows such as `C2CConnect` include AP teardown, wifi setup, reboot trigger, and config signaling in a single function.
detail.suggestion: Separate WiFi domain service (state + device operations) from API adapters (websocket/captive endpoints), and encapsulate mutable runtime state in a struct instance rather than package globals.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::w.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Critical file-open errors are ignored, creating nil-dereference and silent-failure behavior instead of predictable error returns.

evidence: `goseg/config/config.go` reopens config with `file, _ = os.Open(confPath)` and later unconditionally defers `file.Close()`, so reopen failures are dropped and can crash or misinitialize configuration.
`goseg/importer/extract.go` uses `tmpFile, _ := os.Open(src)` then `defer tmpFile.Close()` and `tar.NewReader(tmpFile)`, so open failures are not surfaced and can panic when counting archive entries.
detail.suggestion: Stop using blank-identifier error drops on I/O; check and wrap open failures with `%w`, and only defer close after non-nil file handles. Add regression tests for missing/unreadable source paths.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Multiple flows log serious runtime errors but still return nil/success, producing inconsistent failure semantics for callers.

evidence: In `goseg/routines/docker.go`, `gracefulShipExit` logs failure from `rt.getShipStatus(piers)` but continues and returns nil, so callers cannot detect inability to determine ship state.
In `goseg/handler/support.go`, `dumpBugReport` logs numerous copy/log-dump errors and still returns nil, yielding partial reports without explicit failure status.
In `goseg/broadcast/broadcast.go`, `minIOPwd, _ = config.GetMinIOPassword(...)` discards credential retrieval errors and continues with empty password output.
detail.suggestion: Define a consistent policy for recoverable vs fatal errors in long-running routines; aggregate non-fatal errors and return a structured error summary (or typed partial-success result) instead of unconditional nil.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Mixed `%w` and `%v` wrapping across packages causes uneven context preservation and prevents reliable `errors.Is/As` handling.

evidence: `goseg/system/system.go` preserves causal chains with `%w` (for example `read virtual memory stats: %w`), but `goseg/routines/docker.go` uses `%v` for similar boundary errors (for example `Failed to get statuses for %s: %v`).
`goseg/handler/handler.go` uses wrapped errors in many handlers, while `goseg/handler/dev.go` often formats with `%v`, creating inconsistent upstream behavior for classification/retry logic.
detail.suggestion: Adopt a package-wide rule: wrap propagated errors with `%w`, reserve `%v` for terminal logging only, and migrate high-traffic boundary functions first (handlers, routines, system).

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Adjacent handler pathways expose failures through incompatible channels (returned error vs log/event side effects), making caller behavior unpredictable.

evidence: `goseg/handler/upload.go` consistently returns wrapped errors to caller (`open upload endpoint ...: %w`), enabling deterministic propagation.
`goseg/system/wifi.go` captive websocket loop mostly logs and continues on decode/connect/write failures, with no propagated error to HTTP layer.
`goseg/handler/startram.go` helper actions frequently log and return from void functions, reporting status via events only, not a shared error-return contract.
detail.suggestion: Standardize handler-facing error semantics: either return typed errors from action handlers and translate once at transport boundary, or formalize an explicit result/event contract used consistently across handlers.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Upload action protocol is duplicated as raw strings across handler and service test, creating boundary drift risk.

evidence: `uploadActionOpenEndpoint` is defined in `handler/upload.go`, while `service_test.go` uses the raw literal `"open-endpoint"` directly.
Routing behavior in `Handle` depends on exact string matching, so contract changes require synchronized edits across packages with no compile-time guard.
detail.suggestion: Move upload action identifiers into a shared typed contract (e.g., constants/type in `uploadsvc` or `structs`) and update handler/tests to consume that single source so mismatches fail at compile/test boundaries earlier.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Upload handler accepts an invalid nil service dependency and detects it only at request handling time.

evidence: `NewUploadMessageHandler` stores the provided `uploadsvc.Service` without validation.
`Handle` starts with `if handler.service == nil` and returns runtime error, meaning dependency boundary correctness is not guaranteed at wiring time.
Current upload service tests cover payload translation only and do not enforce constructor-level dependency invariants.
detail.suggestion: Enforce dependency validity at construction (panic/error-returning constructor or non-nil concrete type boundary) and add tests asserting that invalid wiring is rejected before runtime message dispatch.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: The `ConfigService` abstraction in ship creation is a single-implementation pass-through that adds indirection but no domain boundary.

evidence: `ConfigService` methods (`Conf`, `UrbitConf`, `UrbitConfAll`, `UpdateUrbitConfig`, `UpdatePiers`) map 1:1 to `config` package functions.

`configService` implementation is only forwarding calls (e.g., `return config.Conf()` and `return config.UpdateConfTyped(config.WithPiers(piers))`) with no validation, caching, policy, or translation.
detail.suggestion: Either remove `ConfigService` and depend on concrete config APIs directly, or make it a true domain port by narrowing it to ship-creation intents (e.g., `AllocatePorts`, `CreateShipConfig`, `PersistShip`) with an implementation that encapsulates config details.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Runtime seam structs are duplicated across modules, creating a broad wrapper chain pattern with high maintenance cost.

evidence: Each module defines large runtime structs of function fields (`wireguardRuntime`, `netdataRuntime`, `minioRuntime`, `urbitRuntime`, `versionRuntime`, `dockerRoutineRuntime`) plus `new*Runtime()` constructors with repeated wiring of globals.

Many exported functions are thin wrappers (`LoadWireguard`, `WriteWgConf`, `LoadNetdata`, `WriteNDConf`, `LoadMinIOs`, `LoadUrbits`, `CheckVersionLoop`, `DockerSubscriptionHandler`) that only call `*WithRuntime(new*Runtime())`.
detail.suggestion: Extract a shared seam strategy: keep seam injection only at subsystem entrypoints, and collapse repetitive `newRuntime + withRuntime` scaffolding into reusable factories or a smaller number of explicit interfaces per subsystem.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: A global `SysConfig` bag leaks through multiple layers, so callers depend on broad mutable state instead of bounded configuration contracts.

evidence: `config.Conf()` returns full global config and is consumed directly in unrelated concerns (auth session state, orchestration, docker health loops), e.g., `auth.Initialize`, `ValidateAndAuthorizeRequestToken`, `routines.check502Loop`.

Modules mix narrow snapshot helpers (`StartramSettingsSnapshot`, `ConnectivitySettingsSnapshot`) with direct global config and per-ship calls (`config.UrbitConf`, `config.UpdateConfTyped`), indicating no stable boundary.
`broadcast.assembleUrbitView` and handler flows repeatedly pull config values from multiple global getters instead of receiving domain-scoped settings objects.
detail.suggestion: Define small read/write ports per domain (auth settings, startram settings, ship runtime settings) and migrate modules from `Conf()`/global bag access to those ports; reserve `Conf()` for internal config package use.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Operation handlers duplicate the same transition/error lifecycle logic instead of using a shared orchestration abstraction.

evidence: `urbit_operations.go` repeats the same pattern across actions: publish `loading`, define local `handleError`, perform action, publish `success`, sleep, clear transition.
`startram.go` and `importer.go` replicate similar manual event-state choreography with repeated status/error emissions.

`click/hark.go` has near-identical command-generation and execution flow in multiple functions (`sendStartramReminder`, `sendDiskSpaceWarning`, `sendSmartWarning`) without shared template abstraction.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared operation runner abstraction (e.g., `RunTransitionedAction`) that standardizes start/error/success/done transitions and timing behavior, plus reusable templating helpers for repeated click/hark command construction.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::miss.summary: Upload routing contract in README is not validated by the visible test suite

evidence: README.md mandates explicit branch-matrix tests for upload routing outcomes and failures.
goseg/handler/upload.go implements multiple routing/error branches (decode, open-endpoint, reset, default unsupported action).
goseg/uploadsvc/service_test.go only tests OpenEndpointRequestFromWsPayload translation and does not verify handler branch behavior.
detail.suggestion: Add deterministic handler-level tests that execute every documented upload branch and assert returned error semantics for each failure path; keep translation tests in uploadsvc but treat routing behavior as a separate contract suite.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::mask.summary: Masked error policy for StarTram API connection failures lacks contract tests

evidence: README.md requires tests for masked external errors to assert both outward redaction and preserved internal cause chain.
goseg/startram/errors.go defines wrapAPIConnectionError via errpolicy.WrapMasked without corresponding assigned tests validating redaction plus errors.Is/Unwrap behavior.
detail.suggestion: Add focused tests for wrapAPIConnectionError that assert user-facing message masking and cause-chain preservation (errors.Is and/or Unwrap), then keep those checks in CI as a stable contract gate.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Upload handler still exposes importer-era terminology at the module boundary

evidence: README.md states upload routing should remain importer-agnostic.
goseg/handler/upload.go returns wrapped context "reset importer" for reset failures, which reintroduces importer coupling into upload-facing semantics.
detail.suggestion: Normalize upload boundary terminology to upload-specific language (for example, "reset upload endpoint/session") and align all branch errors to one outward policy so boundary contracts stay consistent and importer-agnostic.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.summary: Drive selection and GroundSeg mountpoint resolution are duplicated across new-ship and import flows instead of centralized.

evidence: `handler/NewShipHandler` scans block devices and decides whether to call `system.CreateGroundSegFilesystem` before ship creation.
`handler/createUrbitShip` re-scans block devices and re-runs the same mountpoint regex check to compute `customDrive`.
`importer/OpenUploadEndpoint` repeats the same `ListHardDisks` + `^/groundseg-\d+$` mountpoint detection logic and separate formatting flag behavior.
detail.suggestion: Extract a single drive-provisioning service (for select/validate/format/mountpoint resolution) and call it from both new-ship and import paths; return a typed result (`mountpoint`, `formatted`, `needs_format`) to remove repeated branching.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::t.summary: Transition/event lifecycle management is hand-coded in many handlers with repeated sleep/clear/error patterns.

evidence: `newship`, `support`, `startram`, and `urbit_operations` each define local error wrappers that publish transition errors, sleep, and clear transitions.
`importer/Reset` and `failUploadRequest` manually emit multiple status transitions in fixed sequences, duplicating lifecycle semantics present in other flows.
Transition keys and completion behavior are encoded as string literals in each handler, creating inconsistent conventions and repeated orchestration logic.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared transition orchestrator API (begin/progress/fail/complete/clear with optional auto-clear timing) and migrate handlers to declarative transition steps rather than inline publish-and-sleep blocks.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::t.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::h.summary: Core handlers combine transport dispatch, domain policy, infrastructure control, and state mutation in single routines.

evidence: `NewShipHandler` handles JSON parsing, key normalization, disk/provisioning checks, validation, and workflow triggering inside one request path.
`handleStartramToggle` mutates config, controls container lifecycle, dispatches nested Urbit payloads, and emits UI events in one routine.
`packPier`/`packMeldPier` combine transition rendering, container orchestration, config persistence, and operational retry/timeout semantics.
detail.suggestion: Split handlers into thin transport adapters plus dedicated application services (ship lifecycle service, startram orchestration service, maintenance service) with explicit command objects and narrower responsibility boundaries.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::h.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::g.summary: Extensive mutable package-level state creates implicit coupling and weakens coherent component boundaries.

evidence: `config` exposes mutable globals (`globalConfig`, `GSContainers`, caches, mutexes) as shared runtime authority.
`docker`, `broadcast`, `system`, `auth`, and `importer` each keep independent global mutable maps/channels/session registries used directly by handlers.
Many flows rely on implicit global state snapshots (`config.Conf()`, `config.UrbitConf`, global transition channels) rather than injected dependencies, making coherent ownership boundaries unclear.
detail.suggestion: Move subsystem state behind explicit service structs and inject them into handlers; keep only immutable package constants as globals and isolate mutable runtime state behind interfaces.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::g.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

subjective_review::goseg/shipworkflow/wo.summary: No design review on record — run `desloppify review --prepare`

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Upload service layer is directly coupled to websocket payload structs.

evidence: `uploadsvc.OpenEndpointRequestFromWsPayload` accepts `structs.WsUploadPayload`, forcing service code to import transport-layer types.

Handler routing in `UploadMessageHandler.Handle` switches transport action strings and then delegates transport-to-domain conversion to `uploadsvc`, so boundary translation responsibility is not isolated at the edge.
detail.suggestion: Move `OpenEndpointRequestFromWsPayload` out of `uploadsvc` into `handler` (or a dedicated transport adapter package). Keep `uploadsvc` accepting only `OpenEndpointRequest` and domain-level types so websocket schema changes do not force service-package edits.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Service contract and concrete importer adapter are merged in one package/file.

evidence: `uploadsvc` exports `Service` but also ships `ImporterService` that directly invokes importer package functions, so the same package owns both abstraction and infrastructure detail.
`handler/upload.go` depends on this mixed package for both action contract (`ActionOpenEndpoint`, `ActionReset`) and runtime behavior (`service.OpenEndpoint`/`Reset`).

`startram/errors.go` shows a separate package-specific boundary wrapper (`wrapAPIConnectionError`) while upload path lacks an equivalent boundary-specific adapter layer, indicating inconsistent boundary ownership across subsystems.
detail.suggestion: Split upload service contract/types from importer-backed implementation: keep `uploadsvc` as contract-only (interfaces, request types, actions), move `ImporterService` to a separate adapter package (for example `uploadsvc/importeradapter`), and wire it at composition/root level.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Upload action contract is split between service constants and handler switch logic.

evidence: `goseg/uploadsvc/service.go` exports `SupportedActions()` and typed action constants, but `goseg/handler/upload.go` separately encodes routing behavior in its own `switch` over `uploadPayload.Payload.Action`.
Adding a new action requires parallel edits in `SupportedActions`, handler `switch`, and README’s required test matrix, which increases boundary drift risk.
detail.suggestion: Move action dispatch into `uploadsvc` (for example, a single `Execute(action, payload)` or action-handler map keyed by `Action`) so handler code only decodes transport input and forwards once; derive supported-actions from the same dispatch table.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Upload service contract and importer adapter are co-located, weakening boundary integrity.

evidence: `goseg/uploadsvc/service.go` defines `Service` and `OpenEndpointRequest` but also concrete `ImporterService` that imports and calls `groundseg/importer` directly.
This arrangement makes the contract package transitively depend on importer infrastructure, reducing modular replaceability for compatibility/policy variants.
detail.suggestion: Split `uploadsvc` into a pure contract layer (actions, request DTO, interface) and a separate adapter implementation file/package for importer-backed behavior; wire concrete implementation in composition root.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::qual.summary: Documented quality gates do not cover all runtime contracts they claim to enforce.

evidence: README requires upload routing matrix tests and masked-error contract tests, but its explicit CI gate description only enforces handler file/test coupling (`goseg/handler/upload.go` vs `goseg/handler/upload_test.go`).

Critical contract code exists outside that gate: `OpenEndpointRequestFromWsPayload` mapping and action definitions in `goseg/uploadsvc/service.go`, plus masking wrapper behavior in `goseg/startram/errors.go`.
detail.suggestion: Extend quality gates so contract changes in `goseg/uploadsvc/service.go` and `goseg/startram/errors.go` require corresponding targeted tests (including `errors.Is`/`Unwrap` assertions for masked errors and dispatch-table parity tests for upload actions).

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::o.summary: Ship/startram/import workflows duplicate UI-transition choreography inside domain operations, creating a repeated mixed-responsibility pattern.

evidence: `createUrbitShip`, multiple urbit operation handlers, and startram handlers all publish transitions/events directly, perform core operations, then sleep and clear status in the same functions.
`runImportPhases` plus `errorCleanup` in importer ties lifecycle execution to websocket transition emission instead of separating orchestration from business actions.
`SupportHandler` interleaves bug-report file work, profiling, upload transport, and transition timing (`time.Sleep`) in one request path.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared operation-orchestrator abstraction (phase runner + standardized transition policy) and keep handlers focused on invoking domain use-cases; move sleep/clear semantics into one reusable transition policy component.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::c.summary: Custom-drive detection/formatting logic is duplicated across new-ship and import entry points with diverging control flow.

evidence: `NewShipHandler` and `createUrbitShip` both walk `ListHardDisks()` and apply `/groundseg-<n>` regex checks.
`OpenUploadEndpoint` in importer repeats the same mountpoint search but encodes result through `NeedsFormatting` and later formatting in `ensureUploadDriveReady`.
The same domain rule (resolve selected drive to usable mountpoint/format requirement) is implemented in multiple places with different error/formatting behavior.
detail.suggestion: Extract a single drive-resolution service returning `{mountpoint, needsFormatting, validationError}` and use it from both new-ship and importer workflows so formatting and error semantics stay consistent.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::r.summary: Failure rollback logic for ship provisioning is copied into separate cleanup routines, increasing drift risk.

evidence: `newship.errorCleanup` and `importer.errorCleanup` both remove configs, update system entries, and delete volumes with near-parallel structure.
Deletion/stop/recovery concerns also appear in `deleteShip`, `togglePower`, `rebuildContainer`, and `urbitCleanDelete` with overlapping but non-identical sequencing.
Rollback behavior is encoded per workflow rather than as reusable compensating actions.
detail.suggestion: Create a shared ship-lifecycle compensation module (idempotent steps for config removal, container teardown, volume/custom-path cleanup, transition emission) and compose it from workflows instead of duplicating procedural cleanup blocks.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::b.summary: Broadcast package combines snapshot building, domain aggregation, transport emission, and global state mutation in one module.

evidence: `broadcast.go` constructs urbit/profile/apps/system views, mutates module globals, queries docker/system/config, and emits websocket updates in the same package.
`runtimeSnapshotForPiers` mutates `docker.ContainerStatList` as a side effect while assembling broadcast snapshots.
`constructSystemInfo` and `assembleUrbitView` are long aggregation functions with cross-domain reads and formatting rules embedded directly.
detail.suggestion: Split broadcast into focused collaborators: snapshot collectors (system/docker/startram), pure view mappers, and a thin publisher; remove side-effectful cross-package mutation from snapshot assembly paths.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::w.summary: WiFi module mixes device control, AP lifecycle, websocket API handling, and shared mutable state without a clear boundary.

evidence: `wifi.go` holds websocket upgrader/client map, captive portal config, wifi polling loop, and nmcli/systemctl command execution in one package-level module.
Global mutable structures (`clients`, `WifiInfo`, `Device`, `ConfChannel`) are read/written across goroutines with partial locking only for C2C mode flag.
Handler layer delegates directly to this broad module (`C2CHandler`), reinforcing a coarse interface instead of a focused service boundary.
detail.suggestion: Split wifi responsibilities into separate components (radio control service, AP/captive service, websocket adapter) with explicit synchronization and narrow interfaces; keep handler-facing API at a use-case level.
