review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: The Wi‑Fi transport adapter owns unrelated layers (websocket transport, command semantics, and process restart policy), blurring subsystem ownership boundaries.

evidence: captiveTransportAdapter manages websocket upgrader/client registry/read loop and also performs command interpretation in `processMessage`.
`processMessage` triggers infrastructure side effects via `connectToWiFi` and `restartGroundSeg`, while `restartGroundSegService` shells out to `systemctl restart groundseg`.
In contrast, uploadsvc/service.go separates command dispatch behind `Executor` and `Service`, highlighting that system package bypasses a comparable application-service seam.
detail.suggestion: Extract a captive portal application service that receives typed commands and owns Wi‑Fi/restart orchestration, while keeping `captiveTransportAdapter` limited to transport concerns (decode/encode, connection lifecycle). Route `processMessage` through that service instead of performing side effects directly.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: Action contract definition is inconsistent and partially duplicated, reducing predictability of top-level behavior ownership.

evidence: wifi transport uses raw string matching (`payload.Payload.Action != "connect"`) with no typed contract.
uploadsvc defines typed actions (`ActionOpenEndpoint`, `ActionReset`) plus `UnsupportedActionError`, but also keeps a separate global `supportedActions` slice and dispatch map in `Executor`.
adapter test asserts mapping by converting action constants to/from strings, showing cross-layer dependence on literal protocol values instead of a single canonical action contract owner.
detail.suggestion: Standardize action handling on a single contract pattern: define typed action enums plus parse/validate helpers at boundary adapters, and make one source of truth for supported actions (derive from dispatch or registration API, not parallel lists).

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Global singleton state plus wide function-bag runtimes creates high indirection without clear domain boundaries.

evidence: `config` exposes mutable globals and does heavy init-time setup in `init()`; many packages read/write through this shared state.
`dockerRuntime` and `routineRuntime` each carry dozens of function fields and mostly re-export package calls, indicating indirection wrappers instead of cohesive service contracts.
Entry points wire subsystems through globals rather than explicit lifecycle-scoped dependencies.
detail.suggestion: Introduce cohesive service interfaces (e.g., ConfigStore, ContainerRuntime, TransitionPublisher) with constructor injection; reduce runtime structs to capability-focused dependencies and phase out direct use of mutable config globals.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Tests depend on mutating package globals, showing production seams are not explicit or isolated.

evidence: `TestWriteWgConfCreateAndUpdate` mutates `config.DockerDir` globally.
Version tests mutate global `config.Architecture`.
Rectify tests replace global channel `leak.LeakChan` to keep handlers unblocked.
detail.suggestion: Move mutable process config and channels behind injectable test fixtures/interfaces so tests can provide local instances without cross-test global rewiring.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Cross-module handoffs use ad-hoc string event types, making integration contracts brittle and hard to evolve safely.

evidence: Transition handlers branch on many raw string constants (`pack`, `toggle`, `register`, etc.) with no compile-time linkage across producers and consumers.
Different modules publish and interpret overlapping event strings independently.
Unknown-type handling is runtime-only logging, so drift is detected late.
detail.suggestion: Define typed transition/event enums and shared payload structs in one contract package; centralize publish/apply helpers so producers and consumers share a single protocol surface.
detail.investigation: Refactored hostapd config generation from one raw multiline literal to composable builder functions per section (interface, mode, WPA, WEP). Added section-level assembly in buildHostapdConfig to make format and validation explicit and testable.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Broadcast assembly mixes mapping with live IO and config mutation reads, obscuring boundary responsibilities and creating N+1 seam behavior.

evidence: `urbitViewMapper.assemble` calls `config.LoadUrbitConfig`, `config.UrbitConf`, `docker.GetContainerStats`, and other runtime lookups while also mapping output fields.
`ConstructPierInfo` already prepares runtime snapshots, but mapper still performs additional per-pier lookups and side-dependent reads.
Data translation and data acquisition are not in one obvious seam, making behavior harder to reason about and test.
detail.suggestion: Split into explicit phases: collect all runtime/config inputs once, then run pure mapping over immutable snapshots; keep IO in collectors and keep mappers deterministic.

review::.::holistic::low_level_elegance:.summary: Transition handlers repeat state-fetch/mutate/publish blocks extensively, increasing branch complexity and maintenance risk.

evidence: Many switch cases in `rectify.go` duplicate `current := broadcast.GetState()`, mutate one field, then `UpdateBroadcast` and `BroadcastToClients`.
Similar state-change + broadcast patterns appear in docker routine handlers and maintenance flows.
The repeated imperative pattern increases chances of inconsistent behavior across event types.
detail.suggestion: Introduce small helpers for transition mutation/publish (or table-driven dispatch) so each case only declares field mapping and optional side effects.

review::.::holistic::low_level_elegance:.summary: Config rendering is a monolithic raw string block that obscures intent and invites formatting drift.

evidence: makeConfig (lines 31-83) validates inputs, then emits a large multi-line literal with embedded placeholders and inconsistent indentation.
The generated content mixes concerns (validation assumptions, defaults, protocol comments, and serialization) in one expression, so a tiny change requires editing an 80+ line literal.
detail.suggestion: Split rendering into small named sections/constants (base radio settings, auth settings, optional legacy section) and assemble with a strings.Builder or text/template; keep validation separate from serialization so each step is easier to reason about and test.

review::.::holistic::low_level_elegance:.summary: writeHostapdConfig relies on implicit package globals, reducing local clarity and testability.

evidence: writeHostapdConfig (line 18) calls makeConfig(wlan, ssid, password) without receiving those values as parameters.
The function’s behavior and output depend on external mutable state rather than explicit inputs, which makes control flow harder to reason about in isolation.
detail.suggestion: Change writeHostapdConfig to accept wlan/ssid/password (or a small typed config struct) as arguments and pass them through explicitly; keep only file I/O inside the writer function.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: The handler package mixes unrelated capabilities (auth/session, ship lifecycle, connectivity, dev operations), obscuring ownership and change boundaries.

evidence: goseg/handler/handler.go includes LoginHandler/LogoutHandler, NewShipHandler, C2CHandler, UnauthHandler, and PwHandler in one file.
goseg/handler/dev.go adds development backup/restore and StarTram reminder controls in the same package, with direct imports of backupsvc, startram, click, config, and system.
goseg/handler/dev_test.go must reset many package-level seams for these mixed responsibilities, signaling weak internal boundaries.
detail.suggestion: Split handler into bounded subpackages/files by capability (e.g., handler/auth, handler/ship, handler/connectivity, handler/devtools) with a thin transport entry layer that dispatches to dedicated services.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: The click package acts as a broad multi-domain facade, flattening distinct domains into one API surface and weakening package role clarity.

evidence: goseg/click/click.go exports unrelated concerns together: FixAcme, desk lifecycle methods, SendNotification, storage link/unlink, RestoreTlon, BackupTlon.
goseg/click/code.go keeps +code cache state and click execution plumbing in the same package API family rather than behind narrower domain modules.
goseg/click/click_test.go validates dispatch via package-level seam overrides, reflecting a large, centralized package surface.
detail.suggestion: Refactor click into explicit domain subpackages (e.g., click/desk, click/notify, click/backup, click/luscode) and keep click root as a minimal composition layer or remove it entirely.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: Background routines combine scheduler lifecycle, policy decisions, and direct infrastructure calls, making directory boundaries in routines ambiguous.

evidence: goseg/routines/backup.go contains infinite-loop scheduling, backup window policy (time checks), and direct backupsvc/config calls in one unit.
goseg/routines/chop.go contains periodic scheduling plus direct docker stats access and chop execution policy.
Both files define package-level seam vars and mutable globals, suggesting routines owns both orchestration and service logic instead of delegating policy/services behind clearer boundaries.
detail.suggestion: Separate routines into scheduler drivers (tick/lifecycle) and injected policy/service components (backup policy, chop policy, infrastructure adapters) so routines directory represents orchestration only.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: Upload action compatibility is governed by duplicated sources of truth instead of one contract surface.

evidence: README.md requires dispatch-table parity for every value in `SupportedActions()`.
goseg/uploadsvc/service.go defines `supportedActions` separately from the executor `dispatch` map, so action support is maintained in two independent structures.
Executor.SupportedActions() filters by `dispatch` presence, which can hide drift between declared and actually routable actions instead of failing fast.
The reviewed adapter test covers only `open-endpoint` mapping and does not enforce whole-action contract completeness.
detail.suggestion: Refactor to a single action registry (action -> handler metadata) that drives both `SupportedActions()` and dispatch, and add a deterministic table-driven contract test that fails when any declared action lacks routing.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: Command-routing contracts are modeled inconsistently across subsystems, reducing architectural predictability.

evidence: goseg/system/wifi_transport_adapter.go routes websocket messages by checking `payload.Payload.Action != "connect"` with a raw literal.
goseg/uploadsvc/service.go formalizes actions via `type Action string` constants and explicit unsupported-action errors.
README.md positions backend boundaries as contract-driven, but these two runtime paths apply different contract styles for similar message-dispatch behavior.
detail.suggestion: Adopt a shared command-contract pattern for websocket action handling (typed action enums + explicit unsupported-action behavior) in system transport paths to match service-layer contract rigor.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: Governance policy is concentrated in README prose instead of being co-located with enforceable contract artifacts near runtime boundaries.

evidence: README.md contains detailed boundary and quality-gate obligations (masked external errors and upload dispatch parity).

goseg/startram/errors.go and goseg/uploadsvc/service.go implement relevant contract surfaces but do not embed adjacent self-checking mechanisms (e.g., package-local invariant assertions or contract-oriented tests in the reviewed file set).
This split makes architectural guarantees depend on external process discipline rather than discoverable ownership at the boundary modules themselves.
detail.suggestion: Move key governance rules into code-adjacent contract artifacts: package docs plus boundary-focused tests that codify the guarantees where the contracts live, leaving README as summary rather than primary policy source.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.summary: Urbit lifecycle helpers are duplicated across packages instead of being owned by one domain service.

evidence: `WaitComplete` in both files has near-identical timeout/poll/retry/status-check flow against `docker.GetShipStatus` with the same failure-counter behavior.
Both files define a `persistShipConf` helper with the same `config.UpdateUrbit` mutation closure (`*conf = shipConf`).
The duplicated helpers are used in operational paths (`ChopPier`/delete flows), so behavior changes can drift between copies.
detail.suggestion: Move wait/persist lifecycle primitives into a single shared Urbit lifecycle component (for example under `chopsvc` or a dedicated `shipworkflow` service) and have handlers call that component instead of carrying their own copies.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::i.summary: Transition/event lifecycle handling is scattered and inconsistently encoded across handlers.

evidence: `SupportHandler` manually emits loading/error/reset transitions with hardcoded deferred sleeps and reset events.
`handleStartramRestart`/`handleStartramToggle` partially use `publishTransitionWithPolicy`, but still embed event-type-specific reset payload forms (`nil`, empty string, or explicit data) inline.
`newship.go` publishes transitions directly (`bootStage`, `error`, `patp`) without a common lifecycle wrapper, unlike other handler flows.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a single transition orchestration abstraction (state machine or typed helper) for start/success/error/reset semantics, and migrate handler actions to declarative transition steps rather than ad-hoc publish/sleep blocks.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::i.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::c.summary: Configuration modules mix unrelated responsibilities (bootstrap, runtime caches, persistence, and external system probing).

evidence: `config/config.go` `init()` performs environment bootstrap, file/key creation, defaults merge, binary hashing, persistence, and starts background channel processing in one package entrypoint.
The same file owns both typed patch API (`ConfPatch`/`With*` options) and legacy raw map mutation (`UpdateConf`), creating two mutation paths to one store.

`config/urbit.go` persistence path (`persistUrbitConfigLocked`) also performs Docker container inspection (`getImageTagByContainerName`) before writing config, crossing config/storage and runtime-infra boundaries.
detail.suggestion: Split config responsibilities into separate components: bootstrapping, repository/persistence, runtime snapshot access, and runtime enrichers (e.g., image tag resolvers). Keep one mutation pathway and make runtime-derived fields explicit outside persistence writes.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: Auth package combines token cryptography, HTTP identity checks, websocket client registry, and session persistence in one module.

evidence: `auth/auth.go` includes token generation/encryption (`CreateToken`, `KeyfileEncrypt/Decrypt`, `AuthToken`) while also managing live connection maps (`AddToAuthMap`, `RemoveFromAuthMap`, `Ws*` helpers).
`ValidateAndAuthorizeRequestToken` mixes HTTP request fingerprinting (`X-Forwarded-For`, `User-Agent`) with token mutation and authorization checks.
Login flow in `handler/handler.go` directly depends on this all-in-one module for both auth decision and websocket/session side effects, tightening coupling.
detail.suggestion: Separate auth into at least three collaborators: token service (crypto + claims), request authenticator (context validation), and session/connection registry (websocket state + persistence). Keep handlers orchestrating these explicit interfaces instead of one omnibus package.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::h.summary: Handler layer performs deep domain workflows and infrastructure mutations instead of delegating to cohesive application services.

evidence: `NewShipHandler` validates protocol payloads, derives keys, resolves drives, and dispatches destructive operations; `createUrbitShip` then manipulates Docker volumes/containers, config state, and StarTram registration.
`StartramHandler` switch branches build inline closures and trigger asynchronous orchestration functions that themselves perform cross-module config/docker/startram operations.
`urbit_operations.go` centralizes many unrelated actions (domain aliasing, export, power/network toggles, backups, chop scheduling), creating a broad command surface in one file.
detail.suggestion: Refactor handlers into thin transport adapters that map request -> command object, with command execution moved into focused application services (ship provisioning, startram orchestration, urbit operations), each with bounded dependencies.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::h.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.summary: Duplicate transition orchestration helpers create parallel lifecycle semantics across modules.

evidence: `goseg/handler/workflow.go` defines generic helpers for publish/error transitions (`publishTransitionWithPolicy`, `runTransitionedOperation`), but `goseg/shipworkflow/startram.go` implements its own transition utility (`publishTransition`) and bespoke per-step transition/error mapping in the same domain.

`goseg/shipworkflow/urbit_operations.go` calls shared transition helpers in some places while also handling local state/cleanup transitions in nearby functions, increasing semantic drift across paths.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a single lifecycle transition service in one package and require all ship workflows (startram + urbit operations) to use it for begin/in-progress/success/error/clear transitions, including common delay/clear policies.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.summary: Pack lifecycle orchestration is duplicated between scheduled routines and ship operations.

evidence: `packPier` and `packMeldPier` in `goseg/shipworkflow/urbit_operations.go` each perform local transition initialization, deferred clear/publish behavior, status checks, and wait loops.
`goseg/routines/pack.go` (`setScheduledPackTimer`) implements similar event/polling/transition patterns for packing operations, with repeated wait and status/error handling logic.
These modules also diverge in naming and local error wrapping details, making behavior consistency hard to guarantee.
detail.suggestion: Extract a common packing lifecycle abstraction (e.g., `PackOperationCoordinator`) that standardizes transition emission, status polling, and clear/error semantics, then wire both immediate and scheduled pack flows through it.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.summary: Importer configuration path mixes orchestration, transport branching, mutation, async dispatch, and reporting responsibilities in one function.

evidence: `configureUploadedPier` in `goseg/importer/importer.go` combines request validation, phase selection, upload extraction, system config reads/mutation, network/wireguard scheduling, transition operations, and cleanup dispatch.

The function also relies on explicit asynchronous task spawning (`go` calls) and transition publishing inline, mixing domain orchestration with external side-effect control rather than delegating to dedicated workflow components.
detail.suggestion: Refactor importer flow into a pipeline: parse/validate input, delegate extraction to a dedicated extractor stage, delegate state transitions to workflow coordinator, and keep side-effect scheduling/cleanup in dedicated services with explicit interfaces.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Runtime seam structs duplicate thin pass-through wrappers across container workflows, creating unearned indirection

evidence: `goseg/docker/runtime_seams.go` defines `wireguardRuntime`, `netdataRuntime`, `minioRuntime`, and `urbitRuntime` with many function fields that simply forward to package-level helpers (for example `writeWgConf: func(rt wireguardRuntime) error { return writeWgConfWithRuntime(rt) }`).
`goseg/routines/runtime_seams.go` repeats a similar pattern for `dockerRoutineRuntime` and `versionRuntime`, again wrapping direct package functions without behavioral translation.

Call paths (`LoadWireguard()`, `wgContainerConf()`, `wgContainerConfWithRuntime`, `dockerSubscriptionHandler(newDockerRoutineRuntime())`) add extra construction and delegation hops before reaching actual logic.
detail.suggestion: Collapse the repeated seam types into a small shared abstraction for dependency injection (or accept direct function calls for non-tested internals), then pass concrete implementations via focused constructor arguments. This reduces duplicate runtime struct shapes, shortens call chains, and keeps seams only where behavior actually changes.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Startram transition payloads are serialized and re-parsed before dispatch, adding avoidable abstraction overhead

evidence: `HandleStartramToggle` builds `jsonData` via `json.Marshal(payload)`, then immediately unmarshals to `wiretramPayload` before calling `dispatchUrbitPayloadFn`, while the original typed value is already available.
This round-trip has no interface-protecting behavior and only adds allocations and potential marshal/unmarshal errors in the control path.
detail.suggestion: Pass the typed `structs.WsUrbitPayload` directly to `dispatchUrbitPayloadFn` and remove the JSON round-trip conversion. Keep serialization only at package boundaries (transport/API), not in an in-process orchestration loop.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Command execution seams in wifi are defined but inconsistently applied, creating inconsistent abstraction boundaries

evidence: `goseg/system/wifi.go` declares `runCommandForWiFi` and `execCommandForWiFi` as indirection points, but internal callers mix direct calls (`runCommand`) with seam-backed calls (`execCommandForWiFi`).
`C2CMode()` uses `runCommand("systemctl", "stop", "systemd-resolved")` directly, bypassing the seam intended for command injection and making runtime behavior harder to substitute in tests.
Tests rely on seam-based interception in related packages, so inconsistent use increases fixture brittleness and makes behavior diverge between test and runtime control paths.
detail.suggestion: Use the injected command function consistently for all subprocess invocations in `system/wifi.go` (including `systemctl` calls and similar control-flow commands), and remove direct `exec.Command`/`runCommand` usage inside this package.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: `handler` package remains a mixed-domain dumping ground instead of domain-focused subpackages.

evidence: In `goseg/handler/dev.go`, development endpoint routing and side effects for backup/chop-style operations are mixed with auth/service handler shims in `goseg/handler/handler.go`.
The packet’s `existing_findings` already flags `goseg/handler/` as a flat directory with 21 files.
detail.suggestion: Create domain-oriented subpackages under `goseg/handler` in stages: (1) create `goseg/handler/auth/`, `goseg/handler/dev/`, and `goseg/handler/ship/`; (2) move `handler.go` responsibilities into `handler/auth` and `handler/dev` (or corresponding domain package facades), then replace with thin delegating types/functions in `handler`; (3) update imports with `rg -n "goseg/handler/(DevHandler|LoginHandler|NewShipHandler|C2CHandler|UnauthHandler)"` then `go test ./goseg/...` and `go test ./...`; (4) once package-level exports are stable, remove now-obsolete exports from parent package and rerun `go test ./goseg/handler/...`.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: `routines` aggregates unrelated periodic jobs and service loops in one package, increasing change friction.

evidence: `goseg/routines/backup.go` (remote/local backup orchestration) and `goseg/routines/chop.go` (chop policy loop) are unrelated lifecycle domains but live in one folder.
The packet structure already flags `goseg/routines/` as `file_count: 21` with a `flat_dirs` warning.
detail.suggestion: Split by operational domain first, then recompose via orchestration: (1) create `goseg/routines/backup/` and `goseg/routines/chop/`; (2) move `backup.go`, `backup_test.go`, and related seam vars from root into `backup/`, and move `chop.go` into `chop/`; (3) add stable package-level wrappers in `goseg/routines` (`StartBackupRoutines`, `StartChopRoutines`) to preserve call sites; (4) run `go test ./goseg/routines/...` and `go test ./...`, then prune/normalize imports with `gofmt` + `go test` for any import-cycle regressions.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Docker domain is overpacked in one package, with startup, lifecycle, storage, networking, and event wiring co-located.

evidence: `goseg/docker/docker.go` contains initialization, container build/planning, start/create/remove/get/list/pull/image/network helpers in a single package unit.
`goseg/docker/events.go` defines process-wide channel event bus primitives in the same package, while `docker.go` already carries broad lifecycle responsibility.
Existing findings for `goseg/docker/docker.go` report high complexity and 13 disconnected function clusters, which is a strong structural signal of mixed concerns.
detail.suggestion: Refactor to subpackages around cohesion boundaries: (1) split `events.go` into `goseg/events` (or `goseg/docker/eventsbus`) and keep only docker-specific event types in `goseg/docker`; (2) split `docker.go` into `goseg/docker/lifecycle`, `goseg/docker/registry`, and `goseg/docker/network`; (3) move and adapt callers in a two-pass approach: first extract read-only query helpers, then mutation/ops helpers; (4) update imports and public API shims in a compatibility layer (`goseg/docker` top-level) and validate with `go test ./goseg/docker/...` then `go test ./...`; (5) verify no package import backflow by running the repo-wide tests.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Captive portal transport and Wi-Fi action execution are co-located, obscuring ownership boundaries

evidence: `goseg/system/wifi_transport_adapter.go` handles websocket upgrades, active-client registry, broadcast loop, payload parsing, and dispatch execution in one adapter.
`goseg/system/wifi_c2c_service.go` implements command execution semantics (`connect` + restart) as a separate layer but is coupled through shared `c2cAction` and package-level globals.
detail.suggestion: Split captive-portal transport and C2C execution into explicit subpackages under `goseg/system/wifi/`: `transport/` for websocket/captive adapter concerns and `actions/` (or `service/`) for command execution semantics. Stage 1: move `wifi_transport_adapter.go` to `goseg/system/wifi/transport/captive_transport_adapter.go` and update package name/imports in callers. Stage 2: move `wifi_c2c_service.go` and its helper factories (`c2cServiceFactory`, `connectToWifiForC2C`, `restartGroundSegForC2C`) to `goseg/system/wifi/service/` and expose an explicit interface contract there. Stage 3: add a small adapter interface in `goseg/system/wifi/` to connect the two packages. Validation: `gosegoseg/system/wifi` package builds/tests via `go test ./goseg/system/...`.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Governance and compatibility policy for upload/startram/system contracts is centralized in root README instead of package-local contracts

evidence: `README.md` codifies API/contract/quality-gate expectations for upload dispatch and masked error behavior.
The upload package exposes `SupportedActions()` and dispatch in `goseg/uploadsvc/service.go`, but package-local governance metadata or test contract anchors are absent in that package folder.
Error masking policy is implemented in `goseg/startram/errors.go` via `wrapAPIConnectionError`, while README-level policy is the only place documenting this contract-level expectation.
detail.suggestion: Introduce package-local governance artifacts and colocate compatibility policy: Stage 1: add `goseg/uploadsvc/GOVERNANCE.md` with contract matrix entries for `SupportedActions()` and dispatch parity, plus expected adapter translations; Stage 2: add `goseg/system/wifi/README.md` and `goseg/startram/GOVERNANCE.md` documenting local policy and boundary assumptions; Stage 3: move or reference only cross-cutting rollout notes from root README and keep package docs as the authoritative source for those boundaries. Update CI docs references if needed and validate with `go test ./goseg/uploadsvc/...`, `go test ./goseg/startram/...`, and the existing `upload-contract-gate` workflow invocation path.

review::.::holistic::api_surface_coheren.summary: `GetContainerRunningStatus` can panic on Docker client init failure instead of returning an error through its exported API.

evidence: `goseg/docker/docker.go` defines `GetContainerRunningStatus(containerName string) (string, error)` and calls `cli, err := dockerclient.New()` with an empty `if err != nil {}` block.
The function then does `defer cli.Close()` and continues using `cli` regardless of `err`, so a failed client construction can dereference a nil pointer before returning an error.
`goseg/routines/docker.go` invokes `GetContainerRunningStatus` expecting `(status, err)` semantics; a panic breaks that contract and removes caller-level recovery.
detail.suggestion: In `GetContainerRunningStatus`, return immediately when `dockerclient.New()` fails (e.g., `return "", fmt.Errorf(...)`) and add a regression test that verifies a nil/failed client path returns an error instead of panicking.

review::.::holistic::api_surface_coheren.summary: Wi‑Fi SSID listing collapses operational errors into empty results, obscuring failures as successful empty scans.

evidence: `goseg/system/wifi.go` exposes `func ListWifiSSIDs(dev string) []string` and on `runCommandForWiFi` error returns `[]string{}` after silently ignoring the error.
The same file’s neighboring API functions (`getWifiDevice`, `C2CConnect`, etc.) return errors, so error-signaling semantics are inconsistent within the same subsystem.
`goseg/system/wifi_transport_adapter.go` binds `listWirelessSSIDs: ListWifiSSIDs`, which forces callers through a no-error callback shape and prevents distinguishing "no results" from "scan failure".
detail.suggestion: Change `ListWifiSSIDs` to return `([]string, error)` and update call sites/adapters in `wifi_transport_adapter.go` and related subsystem code to propagate scan failures explicitly; update tests to assert failure observability.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Root packages in handler/docker/routines remain overly broad and should be decomposed by domain.

evidence: blind packet flags "flat_dirs" for goseg/handler (21 files), goseg/docker (20 files), and goseg/routines (20 files)
file-count and directory-profile data indicates these are large mixed directories rather than cohesive bounded packages
assigned files show thin orchestration entrypoints spread across multiple operational concerns instead of narrower feature folders
detail.suggestion: Stage a structural split: (1) introduce feature-oriented subpackages `goseg/handler/dev`, `goseg/handler/ship`, and `goseg/handler/system` (or reuse existing svc namespaces), then move `goseg/handler/dev.go` + `goseg/handler/dev_test.go` into `goseg/handler/dev` and `goseg/handler/handler.go` into a narrow `goseg/handler/api` package; (2) split background runners into `goseg/routines/backup`, `goseg/routines/chop`, and move `backup.go`/`chop.go` + tests there; (3) split docker surface into `goseg/docker/orchestration` and `goseg/docker/config` and move root wrappers like `goseg/docker/docker.go` responsibilities to orchestration edge package; (4) update all imports in callers (e.g., websocket/runner/bootstrap paths) and run `go test ./goseg/handler/...`, `go test ./goseg/routines/...`, `go test ./goseg/docker/...` and `go test ./...` for compile/build validation.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: The handler package still combines public API facades with dev-routing test seams, weakening package ownership clarity.

evidence: `handler.go` re-exports login/auth/c2c handlers and wire-level constants while `dev.go` hosts dev-only orchestration seam wiring, indicating multiple distinct responsibilities inside one package namespace

`dev.go` defines injectable package-level variables (`listHardDisksForDev`, `confForDev`, etc.), showing test seam behavior that belongs to a lower-level domain package rather than core handler aggregation
blind packet records this package as flat (21 files), consistent with mixed boundaries
detail.suggestion: Stage migration: create `goseg/handler/api` for websocket/socket-facing re-exports and `goseg/handler/devsvc` for dev workflow handlers; move `handler.go` to `api` and `dev.go`/`dev_test.go` to `devsvc`, then change callsites to import `goseg/handler/api` for public handlers and `goseg/handler/devsvc` for dev entrypoints; run `go test ./goseg/handler/... && go test ./goseg/...` after updating package names and seam resets.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Routines package is used as a mixed catch-all for unrelated periodic workflows.

evidence: `backup.go` and `chop.go` each own unrelated scheduler behaviors (remote/local backup vs chop loop), yet both sit in same package root with many other domains
directory profile size for `goseg/routines` (20 files) and flat-dir warning indicates weak cohesion across workflow domains
tests already isolate concerns (`chop_test.go` only covers chop scheduling semantics), suggesting better domain partitions are practical
detail.suggestion: Stage split by workflow domain: create `goseg/routines/backup`, `goseg/routines/chop`, `goseg/routines/system` and move each public entrypoint (`TlonBackupRemote`, `TlonBackupLocal`, `StartBackupRoutines`, `StartChopRoutines`, `ChopAtLimit`) into the corresponding subpackages while keeping only a tiny scheduler registry in `goseg/routines`. Update imports in boot code/tests and run `gofmt` + `go test ./goseg/routines/...` and `go test ./...`.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Docker config resolution performs lifecycle mutation, blurring config and orchestration boundaries.

evidence: `container_config.go` case `"minio"` calls `DeleteContainer(containerName)` inside `containerConfigForType` before returning container config, which is a runtime side-effect in a configuration resolver

`docker.go` exposes lifecycle wiring (`configureSubpackages`, client factories, registry hooks) in the same package, reinforcing an already broad boundary across config, lifecycle, networking, and registry concerns
detail.suggestion: Stage a boundary fix: move container cleanup policy into an explicit orchestration path in `goseg/docker/orchestration` (e.g., `StartContainer`) and keep `container_config.go` as a pure config factory with no side effects; rename or remove side-effectful entrypoints and update callers in `goseg/docker/docker.go` + tests, then run `go test ./goseg/docker/...` and `go test ./goseg/...`.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Upload adapter translation tests cover only one action, weakening contract boundary assurance.

evidence: `goseg/uploadsvc/service.go` defines two supported actions (`ActionOpenEndpoint`, `ActionReset`).

`goseg/uploadsvc/adapters/upload_payload_test.go` only verifies `ActionOpenEndpoint` payload mapping and does not exercise `ActionReset` or unsupported-action transport behavior through the adapter layer.
README contract text treats upload command behavior as a compatibility boundary, so incomplete adapter-level action coverage creates a governance blind spot for that boundary.
detail.suggestion: Stage 1: in `goseg/uploadsvc/adapters/upload_payload_test.go`, convert to table-driven tests covering `open-endpoint`, `reset`, and unsupported action cases, asserting command/result fields per action. Stage 2: align with service parity by asserting adapter output for each supported action plus malformed payload rejection. Stage 3: enforce by extending contract gate expectations to include adapter payload contract tests when adapter mapping changes. Validation commands: `go test ./goseg/uploadsvc -run TestCommandFromWsPayloadMapsActionAndRequest` and `go test ./goseg/uploadsvc -run TestExecutorSupportedActionsMatchesContract`; then run `go test ./goseg/handler/ws -run TestUpload*`.

review::.::holistic::package_organizatio.summary: Wi-Fi C2C action table is exported as mutable shared state across package layers.

evidence: `goseg/system/wifi/service/c2c_service.go` exports `C2CActionBindings` as a package-level mutable slice.
The contract boundary docs require adapter layers to remain simple delegators, but a mutable slice increases accidental coupling risk between contract owner and adapter.
The adapter path now depends on the shared binding table shape at runtime rather than a narrow protocol method, so table evolution risk is enforced structurally instead of by encapsulated API.
detail.suggestion: Stage 1: in `goseg/system/wifi/service/c2c_service.go`, make binding table private (`c2cActionBindings`) and expose `SupportedC2CActions() []C2CAction` plus a private `findBinding(action)` helper. Stage 2: update `goseg/system/wifi_c2c_service.go` and any callers to rely only on exported protocol methods, not shared table aliasing. Stage 3: add tests in `goseg/system/wifi`-side contract tests for action parsing, table parity, and unknown-action rejection using the new helpers. Validation commands: `go test ./goseg/system -run TestParseC2CAction` and `go test ./goseg/system -run TestC2CActionBindingsCoverKnownActions` after refactor, then `gofmt` touched files and run `go test ./...` for boundary sanity.

review::.::holistic::initialization_coup.summary: Docker package wires subpackages during import via init(), coupling startup order and hidden global side effects.

evidence: `goseg/docker/docker.go` defines `init()` that calls `configureSubpackages()` and mutates package-level singletons (`lifecycle.SetClientFactory`, `SetContainerConfigResolver`, `SetImageInfoLookup`, `SetImagePuller`, plus network/registry factories).
This happens as soon as package is imported, before consumers can configure or replace collaborators.
detail.suggestion: Replace package `init()` wiring with an explicit `Initialize()`/`New` bootstrap path that accepts injectable factories, so docker subpackages are configured intentionally from application entrypoint code rather than import time.

review::.::holistic::initialization_coup.summary: Dev handler caches process args and derived backup root at package init, creating stale state when config changes later.

evidence: `isDev` is initialized from `checkDevMode()` at declaration time using `os.Args`.
`BackupDir` is initialized from `backupsvc.ResolveBackupRoot(config.BasePath)` once at import time.
Tests compensate by directly mutating globals (`isDev`, `BackupDir`, and dependency seams), indicating production behavior also depends on mutable package state.
detail.suggestion: Move these values behind provider functions (e.g., `isDevFn`, `backupDirFn`) and resolve them during `DevHandler` invocation or explicit app initialization so runtime config and flags are interpreted at call time.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: MinIO container setup ignores cleanup errors, so configuration can continue after failed container teardown.

evidence: In `containerConfigForType` (`containerType == "minio"`), `DeleteContainer(containerName)` is called and its error return is discarded.
Downstream returns from `minioContainerConf` are still used even if delete already failed, so callers cannot distinguish setup failure from success.
detail.suggestion: Capture and return `DeleteContainer` errors before continuing (`if err := DeleteContainer(...); err != nil { return ..., err }`) and document expected MinIO restart semantics.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: System power/restart command failures are dropped, so callers can receive success while critical host operations did not execute.

evidence: `goseg/handler/systemsvc/system.go` creates command runners and calls `cmd.Run()` without checking returned error for restart/shutdown branches (`...groundseg` and `shutdown` cases).
Same pathway publishes transition events before and during command execution in `handler/systemsvc/system.go`, so externally visible state can be misleading.
detail.suggestion: Capture and return `Run()` errors from command execution, and only publish terminal-success transition states once command invocation is confirmed; map failures to explicit error transitions.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Importer directory restructuring suppresses filesystem and walk errors, allowing silent partial migrations that can break restore/import determinism.

evidence: `_ = filepath.Walk(...)` intentionally discards traversal failures (`goseg/importer/importer.go:676`).
Directory creation and rename operations are also called with ignored errors in the same path (`goseg/importer/importer.go:702-703`, `710`, `721-724`).
The function proceeds to subsequent rename/structure steps even when previous operations fail, returning only later generic errors.
detail.suggestion: Handle each `Walk`, `MkdirAll`, and `Rename` error explicitly, fail fast on first critical error, and introduce rollback/cleanup behavior or clear partial-state markers when restructuring starts.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Click subpackages use layered wrappers with minimal added behavior, creating abstraction overhead without clear policy or contracts.

evidence: Top-level click facade functions are one-line delegations to subpackage functions and do not enforce invariants (`goseg/click/click.go:38-93`).

The same execute+create/delete+filter pattern is duplicated across modules rather than normalized into a coherent strategy (`goseg/click/command_exec.go:5-24`, `goseg/click/restore/restore.go:18-37`, `goseg/click/storage/storage.go:12-47`, `goseg/click/notify/notify.go:65-84`).
detail.suggestion: Collapse the duplicate execution pipeline into a shared command primitive and expose a smaller typed API that encodes policy (retry, auth, validation, cache invalidation) at one boundary.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: Access-point startup logic disregards command results and always reports success, so startup state can be inconsistent when command steps fail.

evidence: Shell command returns are assigned to `res, err` but never checked before continuing (`goseg/accesspoint/router.go:22-74`).
The function returns `true` unconditionally (`goseg/accesspoint/router.go:75`) even after failures in interface, iptables, dnsmasq, and hostapd setup.
detail.suggestion: Add strict error checks for each command, propagate first failure, and centralize start-up step orchestration with rollback/cleanup for partially configured networking state.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::back.summary: Critical backup service orchestration in `backupsvc` lacks package-local automated test coverage in this code slice, despite mutation-heavy behavior.

evidence: Batch file list references `goseg/backupsvc/service.go` but no corresponding `goseg/backupsvc/*_test.go` exists in scanned production tree test inventory.
Service operations are invoked by ship-level handlers and touch filesystem/scheduling behavior (`goseg/backupsvc/service.go`, `goseg/handler/ship/backup.go:24-30`).
detail.suggestion: Add unit tests for backup root creation, local backup flow, upload flow toggles, and error-path handling; add a small integration-style test using test doubles for the execute/storage boundaries.

responsibility_cohesion::goseg/backupsvc.summary: 5 disconnected function clusters (11 functions) — likely mixed responsibilities

test_coverage::goseg/click/click.go::unt.summary: Untested critical module (255 LOC, 0 importers) — high blast radius

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Importer workflow failures can be dropped because async execution ignores returned errors from upload processing.

evidence: `HTTPUploadHandler` starts `configureUploadedPier` in a goroutine instead of awaiting the result.
Inside `configureUploadedPier`, `runImportedPierWorkflow` is invoked and its returned error is ignored, so failures are not surfaced to caller-facing status handling.
detail.suggestion: Run import workflow synchronously and propagate the resulting error to request-level handling, or return errors through a bounded channel with an explicit completion/error path so upload status transitions and failure signals are based on actual workflow outcomes.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Timeout handling is determined by string matching on `err.Error()`, creating brittle and non-authoritative error contracts.

evidence: The importer maps outcomes with a substring check (`strings.Contains(err.Error(), "timed out")`), then emits a specific status code.
This makes behavior depend on textual formatting rather than typed sentinel/wrapped errors and can silently misclassify unrelated errors that happen to contain that phrase.
detail.suggestion: Return typed sentinel errors (e.g., `ErrRequestTimeout`) from workflow/execution layers and switch on concrete error values with `errors.Is/As`, keeping message text as diagnostic detail only.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Filesystem rewrite path reports success while ignoring writer flush failures.

evidence: In `CreateGroundSegFilesystem`, writes to the temp mount file use `bufio.Writer` with per-line error checks, but `writer.Flush()` is not checked before success is returned.
In the same area, errors are partially handled during write but final durability errors can be dropped, allowing corrupted or partially written output to be treated as success.
detail.suggestion: Check and return `writer.Flush()` errors (and defer/validate file close) so failed buffered writes fail the operation instead of being reported as successful.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Core system helper silently downgrades I/O parse errors to a zero-value success result.

evidence: `GetTemp` logs an error to stdout on read/parse failure and returns `0` instead of returning or propagating an error.
The function exists in a subsystem where similar operations normally report errors, causing callers to interpret a malformed/failed read as a valid temperature reading.
detail.suggestion: Refactor `GetTemp` to return an error return value and preserve the distinction between real zero-value data and failed reads; update callers to handle that error explicitly.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Command action dispatch patterns are reimplemented per module, increasing the chance of cross-module contract drift as websocket actions evolve.

evidence: `uploadsvc/service.go` uses a private `actionBindings` slice with `parseAction`, `SupportedActions`, and `DescribeAction` to validate and execute actions.
`system/wifi/service/c2c_service.go` mirrors that shape with `c2cActionBindings`, `ParseC2CAction`, and `SupportedC2CActions`.
The adapter test asserts mapping details directly against `uploadsvc.OpenEndpointRequest`/`ActionOpenEndpoint`, tightly coupling test-level expectations to a specific internal action shape.
detail.suggestion: Extract a shared command-contract abstraction for websocket action registries (including canonical action metadata, parsing, supported-action enumeration, and operation description), then implement module-specific adapters against that boundary to prevent duplicated contract logic and detect drift when action sets change.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Broadcast uses mutable package-level function/variable seams that leak test-only indirection into production behavior.

evidence: `broadcast.go` exposes mutable globals used as seams (for example loop-related helpers and configurable constructors) that are reassigned in tests, making runtime behavior depend on shared mutable package state.
`leak/broadcast.go` also maintains package-global channels/state (`BytesChan`, status sink structures) and ad-hoc sender wiring instead of explicit instance-scoped collaborators.
The seam pattern is uneven across files in the same area, so some paths go through helpers while others call concrete implementations directly.
detail.suggestion: Replace process-global seam vars with an explicit `BroadcastRuntime` interface and inject an instance into loop/sender startup. Keep only immutable dependencies in `loop` and `send` structs, and pass sink/state per call path so tests can substitute mocks without mutating package-level globals.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Runtime seam abstractions are duplicated across packages with low leverage and identical intent.

evidence: `routines/runtime_seams.go` defines a large function-field-backed runtime adapter pattern (`dockerRoutineRuntime`-style struct) that mirrors seams in `docker/orchestration/runtime_seams.go`.
Both files provide test-oriented overrides instead of small, behavior-bearing interfaces, increasing indirection layers without introducing reusable policy or translation logic.
Callers still reach through package-specific wrappers for similar concerns, so the abstraction duplication does not reduce coupling across subsystem boundaries.
detail.suggestion: Consolidate shared seam concerns into a single typed seam package or minimal interfaces colocated at subsystem boundary; remove duplicated adapter stacks and inject dependencies explicitly at startup. Prefer behavior-rich interfaces (one method per responsibility) over function-bag structs.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Config is passed as broad bags, forcing callers into brittle field assertions and weakening API boundaries.

evidence: Config domain types in `structs/configs.go` expose loosely typed fields/structures that downstream modules then downcast and inspect directly.
Callers in importer/shipworkflow/lifecycle pass broad config/context objects through multiple layers rather than explicit capability-specific options.
This pattern appears repeatedly (`container`, `wireguard`, and workflow paths) and ties unrelated package internals to implementation-level keys.
detail.suggestion: Introduce domain-specific option interfaces/types for each operation (e.g., `VersionOptions`, `LifecycleConfig`, `WireguardOptions`) and pass only required fields. This removes repeated assertions and clarifies each abstraction boundary.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Upload and C2C modules duplicate command-contract plumbing, increasing cross-module contract drift risk.

evidence: `uploadsvc/service.go` defines `Action`, `parseAction`, `actionBindings`, and `SupportedActions` to model protocol actions.
`system/wifi/service/c2c_service.go` defines `C2CAction`, `ParseC2CAction`, `c2cActionBindings`, and `SupportedC2CActions` with the same contract pattern.
Both are governed by the same compatibility/runtimes contract intent in `README.md` but have no shared contract abstraction.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared command-contract helper in a dedicated package (for example `goseg/contracts/ws`), with typed action metadata and common parser/dispatcher adapters, then convert both modules to consume the shared registration and describe operations from a single source of truth to prevent independent drift.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::miss.summary: Contract execution paths for upload and Wi‑Fi actions are under-tested despite runtime gate expectations.

evidence: The only upload test in scope checks payload mapping, but does not exercise `Executor` dispatch, unsupported action behavior, nil service handling, or `DescribeAction` in `uploadsvc/service.go`.
The Wi‑Fi C2C service includes JSON unmarshalling, action parsing, and dispatch in `ProcessC2CMessage`, but no C2C contract tests exist in the scoped files.
README’s governance expectations call out deterministic/unit verification for upload and websocket contract flows.
detail.suggestion: Add focused tests for `uploadsvc.Executor` and `system/wifi/service.C2CService`: test happy path, unsupported actions, malformed payloads, nil/invalid service factories, and operation-descriptor paths to pin contract behavior and satisfy command-compatibility expectations.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::c.summary: Split `config` into boot/runtime state and feature-specific config writers to avoid mixed responsibilities

evidence: `goseg/config/config.go` owns process bootstrap (`init()`, `initializeConfig`, `initializePaths`), mutable global runtime state (`globalConfig`, `BasePath`, `Conf()`, multiple mutexes), persistence (`persistConf`, `UpdateConfTyped`), and domain snapshots for every subsystem.

`goseg/config/mc.go` and `goseg/config/version.go` mutate feature-specific config files by directly reading global config state (`BasePath`, `confForMc`, `getVersionChannelForMc`) and performing file I/O in the same package.
A single change in config loading or storage layout requires touching global and feature behavior in the same module family, making separation and testability weaker.
detail.suggestion: Introduce explicit boundaries: a `configbootstrap` module for startup and env/path setup, a `configstore` module for typed config persistence/state synchronization, and per-feature adapters (`mc`, `version`) that receive dependencies rather than reaching into globals.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::c.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::s.summary: Refactor bug-report HTTP handler into a dedicated coordinator plus smaller domain services

evidence: `SupportHandler` performs request decode, path initialization, artifact collection (`dumpBugReport`), optional CPU profiling, archive creation, HTTP upload (`sendBugReport`), status transitions, and sleep-based transition emissions in one flow.
The same file also contains low-level helpers for zip creation, JSON sanitization, path selection, and file system cleanup, tightly coupling API handling with transport and packaging internals.
This coupling makes handler behavior hard to reason about and difficult to unit-test without broad side effects.
detail.suggestion: Create a `support` domain service with explicit steps: `SupportPayload` validation, `BugReportBundleAssembler`, and `BugReportTransport`, and keep `SupportHandler` as only request binding and status orchestration.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::s.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: Separate auth token crypto, transport request handling, and session persistence into distinct components

evidence: `goseg/auth/auth.go` mixes token encryption/decryption (`KeyfileEncrypt`, `KeyfileDecrypt`), HTTP/WebSocket identity extraction (`requestIdentityFromRequest`), in-memory websocket map operations (`ClientManager`, `WsIsAuthenticated`, `WsNilSession`), and durable session persistence (`AddSession` calling config updates).
`ValidateAndAuthorizeRequestToken` and `CreateToken` combine HTTP request identity parsing, crypto, session map updates, and storage updates in a single file path.
The persistence touchpoints (`AddSession` -> `config.UpdateConfTyped`) force auth changes to couple directly to config implementation details.
detail.suggestion: Extract auth into: (1) transport/context adapter, (2) token crypto strategy, (3) session-state repository for persistence, and keep websocket map updates in a dedicated connection lifecycle service.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.summary: Centralize mountpoint/media-path selection to avoid divergent mmc/data placement behavior

evidence: `goseg/handler/system/support.go:makeBugReportPath` scans `gopsutil.disk.Partitions` and walks parent directories with `path.Dir` to infer mmc/media placement.
`goseg/logger/logger.go:makeLogPath` performs the same outer-loop mountpoint + `strings.Contains(p.Device, "mmc")` decision path with slightly different defaults.
`goseg/config/config.go:GetStoragePath` and `checkIsEMMCMachine` use another independent variant of the same environment and mountpoint checks.
`goseg/system/disk.go:IsMountedMMC` duplicates another traversal variant (`listPartitions` + parent loop) for storage placement.
These copies diverge in fallback values and absolute-path handling, so future policy changes can create silent inconsistencies.
detail.suggestion: Extract a shared storage placement service (e.g., `platform/storage_path.go`) with a single tested API for base path, media-path resolution, and fallback strategy, then use it across these modules.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::r.summary: Replace ad-hoc readiness polling loops with a shared container wait abstraction

evidence: Both MinIO and netdata container config builders repeatedly poll `getContainerRunningStatus("wireguard")` in local loops waiting for status text containing `"Up"`.
The loop shape (status fetch, contains-check, sleep/poll) is repeated instead of shared behind one utility with timeout/error semantics.
The orchestration layer now has multiple bespoke timing and blocking strategies, which increases drift across service startup behavior.
detail.suggestion: Add a testable `ContainerReadiness` utility (with timeout and backoff) and route MinIO/netdata/volume-writer startup dependencies through it to standardize behavior and eliminate duplicated loop control code.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::r.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: WireGuard config load ignores `ReadAll` error, allowing JSON decode on partial or empty bytes without surfacing root cause

evidence: goseg/config/wireguard.go:34 reads file contents with `byteValue, _ := ioutil.ReadAll(configFile)` and does not check the I/O error.

goseg/system/system.go has similar silent failures in the same subsystem flow (e.g., `files, _ := ioutil.ReadDir(labelDir)` and `resolvedPath, _ := os.Readlink(fullPath)`) where partial/empty results are later consumed without fault propagation.
Both sites return normal values on malformed read-path outcomes, so callers cannot distinguish invalid input from normal data.
detail.suggestion: Handle all I/O errors explicitly: return wrapped errors from `GetWgConf()` and disk-label helpers, and fail fast or fallback only with explicit and consistent error semantics.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Broadcast state enrichment discards desk code retrieval failures and treats them as empty values, masking runtime regressions

evidence: goseg/broadcast/broadcast.go:280 assigns `lusCode, _ := click.GetLusCode(pier)` and returns `lusCode` without surfacing lookup errors.

Nearby callers in `system/system.go` also convert lookup failures to silently empty values in label and mount resolution paths, creating inconsistent error behavior across related status-reporting helpers.
This means callers cannot tell whether missing data is due to a genuine empty state or an upstream error.
detail.suggestion: Change `lusCodeIfRunning` to return `(string, error)` and propagate or explicitly annotate non-fatal failures; ensure callers preserve a consistent contract when `click.GetLusCode` fails.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Container discovery failures are treated as non-existent containers, enabling wrong control flow in lifecycle management

evidence: goseg/docker/lifecycle/lifecycle.go:240 uses `existingContainer, _ := FindContainer(plan.Name)` and proceeds as if absent when discovery fails.
goseg/docker/lifecycle/lifecycle.go:386 similarly ignores lookup errors before deleting/recreating MinIO containers.
goseg/docker/orchestration/minio.go:203 ignores `rt.getContainerRunningStatus("mc")` and can loop indefinitely while always treating status as unavailable due transient failures.
detail.suggestion: Handle `FindContainer`/`getContainerRunningStatus` errors explicitly: abort lifecycle/recovery operations on real discovery faults, and in the MinIO wait loop return a bounded timeout or failure instead of infinite polling.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Initialization ignores embed sub-filesystem setup error, leaving HTTP file serving with a potentially zero-value FS

evidence: goseg/main.go:58 declares `webContent, _ = fs.Sub(content, "web")` and ignores the returned error.
Subsequent `http.FileServer(http.FS(webContent))` uses that value immediately, so an embed path mismatch would not fail deterministically at startup.
detail.suggestion: Capture and handle `fs.Sub` error in `init`/startup; log it and fail startup if web assets are not available to avoid serving a broken web handler.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::b.summary: Broadcasts authorized state twice and checks marshal error too late

evidence: `BroadcastToClients` calls `auth.ClientManager.BroadcastAuth(authJson)` before `err` is checked, then checks error and calls `BroadcastAuth` again at `goseg/broadcast/broadcast.go:432-437`.
`runBroadcastTick` invokes `BroadcastToClients` every tick when auth/clients are present, so duplicates are repeatedly emitted (`goseg/broadcast/loop.go:9-13`, `goseg/broadcast/loop.go:24-29`).
detail.suggestion: Marshal to JSON first, return on error, and call `BroadcastAuth` exactly once with the serialized payload. Add a regression test in `goseg/broadcast` for single-send behavior on each tick.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::b.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Broadcast package acts as a cross-domain orchestrator instead of a focused boundary

evidence: `broadcast.go` imports `auth`, `backupsvc`, `click`, `config`, `docker`, `leak`, `startram`, and `system` together (`goseg/broadcast/broadcast.go:6-15`).
The package both owns domain state assembly and publishes transport events (`BroadcastToClients`, leak sink wiring, global maps/channels).
Routers/handlers consume handlers from multiple domains while broadcast remains a central fan-out coordinator (`goseg/handler/router/leak.go:14-22`, `goseg/handler/ship/urbit.go:1-3`).
detail.suggestion: Split broadcast into explicit layers: a collector layer that only builds immutable snapshots and a publishing layer that handles transport; inject collaborators via narrow interfaces so the package no longer depends on unrelated domain modules.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Malformed click CLI responses can panic due to unchecked string slicing

evidence: Both `filterResponse` variants do `line[:endIndex]` before verifying `endIndex` (`goseg/click/filter.go:28-33`, `goseg/click/internal/runtime/runtime.go:79-84`).
The parser is used in high-throughput command execution paths that should fail safely on bad output (`goseg/click/notify/notify.go:65-82`, `goseg/click/internal/runtime/runtime.go:108-120`).
detail.suggestion: Create a shared parser that validates `]` existence before slicing and returns explicit parse errors, then route both parser callsites through it.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Duplicate response parser implementations create fragile, inconsistent click behavior

evidence: Nearly identical parse branches for `success`, `code`, and `desk` live in both parser functions (`goseg/click/filter.go:17-61`, `goseg/click/internal/runtime/runtime.go:66-106`).
Both modules expose separate surfaces while producing same outcomes, increasing maintenance cost and the chance of divergence.
detail.suggestion: Collapse parser behavior into one internal package-level helper used by both files, then add table-driven tests for malformed and edge-case lines.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Filesystem cleanup in Hoon file helpers swallows failure states

evidence: `deleteHoon` ignores `os.Stat` errors other than `os.IsNotExist` and ignores `os.Remove` errors completely (`goseg/click/hoon.go:40-42`).
The same pattern exists in `runtime.DeleteHoon` (`goseg/click/internal/runtime/runtime.go:34-40`), so multiple call chains inherit silent failure behavior.
detail.suggestion: Return errors from `DeleteHoon` and `create/execute` paths, then propagate or log at callers where cleanup failures should affect command/error semantics; at minimum handle and report unexpected `Stat`/`Remove` failures.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Bug report upload drops server error payload when status is non-200

evidence: On non-200 responses, `sendBugReport` formats error with `%s` and `resp.Body` instead of reading body bytes (`goseg/handler/system/support.go:419-420`).
This discards actionable response details and can make upload failures harder to debug.
detail.suggestion: Read and close the response body into a bounded buffer on non-success, wrap with status code, and add request timeout/response-body assertions in tests.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Parallel runtime seam abstractions are redefined in multiple packages, creating maintenance drift and inconsistent dependency policy.

evidence: `routines/runtime_seams.go` and `docker/orchestration/runtime_seams.go` both define base/runtime structs with duplicated function-pointer dependency members and constructor wiring over the same configuration-backed sources.
`routines/docker.go` and orchestration modules each rely on these duplicated seams independently, so a policy or dependency change must be replicated across files instead of applied centrally.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared seam abstraction layer (for example a small `runtime_dependencies` package) with reusable builder helpers and a minimal cross-package interface for container orchestration/config/logging/test seams; wire package-specific fields as composed overlays instead of duplicating full runtime scaffolding.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Docker event listener/subscriber routines have no explicit stop contract, so background lifecycle control is pushed into global goroutine orchestration rather than the abstraction interface.

evidence: `DockerListener` creates `ctx := context.Background()` and calls `cli.Events(ctx, ...)` while `main.go` launches it as a fire-and-forget goroutine (`go routines.DockerListener()`) with no cancellable handle.
The function loops forever on `select { case event := <-messages; case err := <-errs }` with no `ctx.Done()` or close-channel handling, so callers cannot express graceful stop/restart boundaries.
Other background subsystems in the same code path (for example `CheckVersionLoop` and `startServer` flow) are also goroutine-spawned without shared lifecycle wiring, increasing subsystem coupling.
detail.suggestion: Refactor `DockerListener` to accept a runtime context and/or stop channel in its constructor/runtime struct; have `main` own a cancellation scope and close it on shutdown paths so tests and orchestrators can deterministically stop/restart listener loops.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Archive extraction paths are split by extension with near-duplicate control logic rather than a shared extraction strategy abstraction.

evidence: `extractUploadedPier` switches on extensions and delegates to separate extractor functions, then each extractor performs similar event-loop/progress behavior inline.

`extractZip`, `extractTarGz`, and `extractTar` each manually reimplement timeout setup, directory creation, entry filtering, output writes, and progress publishing details with slight behavioral drift.
Progress semantics differ between implementations (`fileCount`/size weighting differs per extractor), making extension handlers harder to reason about as independent policy changes evolve.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a common extraction strategy interface with shared archive traversal helpers and a single progress publisher; keep format-specific handlers focused only on parsing/writer-specific reader behavior to reduce duplicated abstraction depth.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: Auth token payload map declares duplicate `token` key, risking invalid compile/contract behavior.

evidence: `CreateToken` sets `"token"` twice in the same map literal, making the entry definition ambiguous and likely a real compile-time defect in Go map construction.
Import path consumers in `importer` expect a single stable token mapping (`Token.ID`, `Token.Token`) and the duplicate obscures intended token output shape.
detail.suggestion: Remove the duplicate key and keep a single canonical `Token` entry; if additional encoded fields are needed, use distinct map keys or a typed payload struct and validate consumers in importer accordingly.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::l.summary: Leak websocket action parser panics on short or malformed packets due unguarded slicing.

evidence: `handleAction` calls `stripped := result[5:]` before validating `len(result)`.
Malformed inputs (malicious or partial network payloads) can cause out-of-range panics and tear down bug-reporting/handler execution paths.
No defensive protocol-length check exists before `reverseLittleEndian`/decode steps.
detail.suggestion: Add explicit length/frame validation in `handleAction` (e.g., minimum bytes + optional checksum/type checks) and return/log malformed payload errors rather than slicing blindly.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::l.summary: Docker polling loop returns after first tick, so recurring lifecycle work never runs.

evidence: `DockerPoller` uses `for { select { case <-ticker.C: ... return } }` which exits on the first tick.
Name and implementation imply a long-lived poller, but current control flow makes it effectively one-shot.
detail.suggestion: Replace `return` with periodic dispatch (or remove `select` entirely and run the poll handler directly in the loop) so the function continuously polls and calls container orchestration reconciliation work.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::m.summary: MinIO account bootstrap is coupled to CLI response text and fails when expected phrasing differs.

evidence: `setMinIOAdminAccountWithRuntime` removes an existing service account and only proceeds to add a new one if command output contains `"successfully"`.
`createMinIOServiceAccount` callers (e.g., `toggleMinIOLink`) interpret this as fatal and cannot enable integration when removal output format differs or no prior account exists.
detail.suggestion: Parse exit codes from `mc` commands directly and make creation idempotent: treat "not found" as non-fatal, attempt add after remove (or create directly) and only fail on explicit command errors.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Decision points ignore container lookup errors and proceed as if conditions are healthy.

evidence: `ensureRunningContainer` uses `existingContainer, _ := FindContainer(plan.Name)` and treats any non-nil error as `nil` state in the switch, skipping the failure signal and potentially creating/starting containers via blind recovery.

`cleanupMinIOContainerForStart` also uses `existingContainer, _ := FindContainer(containerName)` and proceeds only on pointer nil, so lookup failures can hide and cause unnecessary delete/start attempts.
`setMinIOAdminAccountWithRuntime` polls `status, _ := rt.getContainerRunningStatus("mc")` and ignores errors, so transient Docker API failures can turn into indefinite waiting loops.
detail.suggestion: Capture and handle these lookup errors explicitly: return/publish a failure path when status/container queries fail, and only retry on explicit transient classes with bounded backoff. In polling loops, add timeout/circuit-breaker and fail with a surfaced operational error instead of looping forever.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Broadcast update returns success when payload marshal fails, masking mutation failure.

evidence: `updateBroadcast` logs `json.Marshal(newBroadcast)` errors and returns `oldBroadcast, nil` rather than propagating an error.
`updateBroadcast` also checks per-recipient `wrapperUrbitBytes, err := json.Marshal(wrappedUrbit)` and only sends on nil, but never returns/report this failure to callers.
Tests in `goseg/leak/broadcast_test.go` validate `updateBroadcast` error behavior but do not observe partial-failure telemetry when marshalling fails, so this silent path is unobservable to callers.
detail.suggestion: Return a wrapped error when either marshal fails, and keep the function output explicit about failed updates (e.g., return unchanged state + error). Also propagate/aggregate per-recipient marshal failures so broadcast callers can react or alert on partial fanout failure.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: C2C payload processing ignores envelope type, weakening protocol boundary enforcement between transport and action dispatch.

evidence: `ProcessC2CMessage` unmarshals into `structs.WsC2cPayload` and dispatches on `payload.Payload.Action` only.
No check exists for `payload.Type == "c2c"` or any namespace/type guard before calling `ParseC2CAction` and `service.Execute(...)`.
Tests assert JSON/action/`nil` factory behavior but do not include an envelope-type mismatch case.
detail.suggestion: Enforce message contract at the C2C module boundary: validate `payload.Type` against the expected namespace/token before action parsing, return a typed unsupported/contract error on mismatch, and add a unit test that a non-`c2c` message is rejected deterministically.
