review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: Exported API contract descriptor is mutable, weakening contract ownership guarantees.

evidence: `goseg/startram/errors.go` defines `APIConnectionErrorContract` as an exported `var`, not a read-only contract object.
The README establishes this file as a governance point of truth for protocol/error contract surfaces; mutability in a governance file undermines that boundary.
detail.suggestion: Make the contract descriptor immutable to callers (e.g., keep contract data in an unexported `var` and expose `func APIConnectionErrorContract() APIConnectionErrorContractDescriptor` or return literal values from dedicated accessors), so external packages cannot alter compatibility policy by assignment.

review::.::holistic::high_level_elegance.summary: Contract compatibility vocabulary is inconsistent across governance surfaces.

evidence: `goseg/protocol/actions/actions.go` exports compatibility constants as `CompatibilityStable`/`CompatibilityBackwardSafe`, while `goseg/startram/errors.go` uses `ErrorContractStable`/`ErrorContractBackwardSafe` for the same concept.
README frames these files as canonical contract definitions, so divergent naming increases cognitive load for maintainers working across protocol and error contract domains.
detail.suggestion: Standardize contract vocabulary and naming (for example, use a single `Compatibility*` terminology across both files, or centralize compatibility aliases in a shared contract package and re-export consistently) to keep governance semantics predictable.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::a.summary: Auth package bundles lifecycle orchestration with token crypto and websocket auth flows in one file, obscuring ownership.

evidence: `goseg/auth/auth.go` owns startup lifecycle (`Initialize`, `Start`, `Stop`, `runAuthCleanupLoop`) and also token serialization/cipher handling (`KeyfileEncrypt`, `KeyfileDecrypt`, `AuthorizeUploadToken`, `CreateToken`).

`goseg/structs/ws.go` also exposes websocket session primitives (`ClientManager`, `MuConn`) used by auth while carrying protocol payload contracts, widening the same domain into low-level transport + policy concerns.
detail.suggestion: Split auth into separate modules (for example `auth/lifecycle`, `auth/token_validation`, and `auth/session`), and keep websocket session primitives in a dedicated structs or session package with explicit interfaces for the auth boundary.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::b.summary: Broadcast file conflates stateful domain model management with transport emission concerns.

evidence: `goseg/broadcast/broadcast.go` defines global mutable state (`broadcastState`, schedule maps, mutexes), transition application helpers, and websocket/leak fan-out entrypoints in the same package surface.
It also handles state cloning and serialization in the same file as transport dispatch (`BroadcastToClients`, `UnauthBroadcast`), creating an intertwined handoff path.
detail.suggestion: Split `broadcast.go` into `state` (durable state machine, cloning, mutation invariants), `transitions` (intent encoding), and `delivery` (broadcast transport + retry/drop policy) to keep handoff seams explicit.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.summary: Runtime seam patterns are reimplemented across docker packages instead of converging on one shared abstraction.

evidence: `goseg/docker/orchestration/runtime_seams.go` and `goseg/docker/lifecycle/lifecycle.go` both define separate `Runtime` types with option adapters and default adapter injection.

`goseg/docker/orchestration/container/helpers.go` and `goseg/docker/orchestration/volume_writer.go` duplicate `writeConfigArtifact`/volume bootstrap wrapper patterns rather than reusing a single internal seam.
`goseg/docker/orchestration/container/netdata.go` composes its own artifact-writing options against these near-equivalent patterns, increasing drift risk.
detail.suggestion: Extract a shared internal runtime/composition primitive for adapter wiring and artifact-plan execution, then migrate both lifecycle and orchestration call paths to it before adding more container-specific runtimes.
detail.investigation:
## Issue 1 Analysis: dc3_volume_bootstrap_pattern_split

- Investigated orchestration volume bootstrapping paths in `netdata.go`, `wireguard.go`, `minio.go`, and `llama.go`.
- Implemented a shared bootstrap primitive in `volume_writer.go`:
  - Added `VolumeInitializationPlan` with explicit volume creation semantics.
  - Centralized copy/write fallback flow in `writeConfigArtifact`.
  - Made volume existence/creation conditional and shared with copy fallback.
- Wired runtime-specific plan configuration through existing `dockerRuntime` seam:
  - `netdata`, `wireguard`, and `minio` now use runtime-provided volume operations for bootstrapping.
  - Llama now uses the same plan constructor via its existing helper.
- Updated tests to use deterministic no-op volume ops in runtime seams where Docker is not available.

## Issue 2 Analysis: 8bd44875

- Reviewed `backup_runtime.go` and identified transport, crypto, and restore-runtime responsibilities coexisting in one file.
- Split runtime behavior into focused files:
  - `startram/backup_transport.go` now owns API call and download verification logic.
  - `startram/backup_crypto.go` now owns AES-GCM encrypt/decrypt helpers.
  - `startram/backup_restore_runtime.go` now owns restore request types and restore-runtime orchestration/volume replay flow.
- Kept orchestration boundary in `backup_service.go` unchanged (interface + orchestration entrypoints) and retained existing public functions by moving implementations out of `backup_runtime.go`.
- Added compatibility coordinator in `backup_runtime.go` to document the new split and avoid a monolithic mixed-concern module.
## Issue 8f Issue 1 Analysis: desk_lifecycle_duplication

- Investigated `goseg/shipworkflow/urbit_operations.go` where install/remove desk handlers for `penpai` and `groundseg` duplicated status/action/wait orchestration.
- The shared flow is: read desk state, branch install/revive/wait for install path, uninstall, then wait for expected running state change.
- Confirmed this flow is currently wrapped only by `runDeskTransition` and duplicated across 4 functions.
- I will replace this with a generic desk lifecycle helper that owns the shared transition flow and accepts per-desk parameters (desk name and operation callbacks), reducing duplicate code and adding a central place for action messages.
## Issue 2 Analysis: routines-capability-blend

- Investigated `routines/disk.go` and `routines/logs.go` against the current finding and confirmed mixed domain responsibilities (disk health + log transport/streaming).
- Implemented domain split under `routines/healthcheck` and `routines/logstream`:
  - Moved existing disk polling/state logic and notification helpers into `routines/healthcheck`.
  - Moved legacy log cleanup/splitting, websocket streaming, and session cleanup logic into `routines/logstream`.
- Replaced `routines` root `disk.go`/`logs.go` with thin delegation functions to preserve exported API used by startup orchestration.
- Relocated corresponding tests to their new packages and kept behavior-equivalent coverage.
## Issue 3 Analysis: bcast_state_loss_on_collector_error

- Investigated `runBroadcastTickWithRuntime` and confirmed the pier collector error path currently allowed `pierInfo` to become empty, which then overwrote in-memory urbit state for that tick.
- Changed failure behavior in `broadcast/loop.go` to retain the last-known `broadcastState.Urbits` when `constructPierInfoFn` returns an error.
- Added regression coverage in `broadcast/loop_test.go` to assert `Transition` and `Info` for existing urbits are preserved across a simulated collector failure.

## Issue 4 Analysis: startup_optional_runtime_handoffs

- Investigated startup runtime handoff in `goseg/startup_orchestrator.go`.
- Introduced explicit runtime contract validation:
  - Added required-callback metadata and validation checks for `startupRuntime`.
  - Added required callback checks for `startBackgroundServicesRuntime`, with `syncRetrieve` conditionally required when `startramWgRegistered == true`.
- Updated bootstrap path:
  - `Bootstrap` now validates the merged startup runtime before task execution.
  - `startBackgroundServicesWithRuntime` now validates background callbacks and returns an error on missing required hooks.
  - Required background callbacks are invoked without silent nil-guarding after validation.
- Added/updated unit tests in `goseg/startup_orchestrator_test.go` to cover:
  - missing startup runtime callbacks rejection,
  - missing background runtime callbacks rejection,
  - expected callback startup sequencing when required hooks are supplied,
  - no-op behavior when startram sync is intentionally disabled.

## Issue 5 Analysis: urbit_conf_snapshot_stale_mutation

- Investigated `urbitContainerConfWithRuntime` in `goseg/docker/orchestration/urbit.go`.
- Fixed stale in-memory config usage by refreshing effective ship config after `UpdateUrbit` writes.
- Switched subsequent container decision logic (`BootStatus`, script path, ports, runtime settings, network mode, mounts) to the reloaded config.
- Added regression coverage in `goseg/docker/orchestration/urbit_test.go` to ensure a config mutation that changes `BootStatus` is honored immediately and drives expected script selection.

## Issue 6 Analysis: c2c_killswitch_no_shutdown_boundary

- Investigated C2C check and lifecycle wiring in `goseg/main.go`.
- Changed C2C check entrypoints to accept a context so kill-switch startup can observe shutdown signals.
- Updated kill-switch loop (`killSwitch`) to accept the same context, use timer+select and return when context is canceled.
- Threaded context through startup callback flow (`StartupOptions.StartC2cCheck`, startup orchestrator background startup path, and main bootstrap wiring) so C2C maintenance stops with application shutdown.
- Added regression test in `goseg/main_test.go` verifying `killSwitch` exits promptly when context is done.
- Evidence confirms config.go and version.go still combine configuration loading, mutation patch construction, runtime snapshots, merge logic, and utility helpers.
- I will split boundaries into focused modules while preserving exported API for callers:
  - loader/parsing: reading raw system.json, default config creation, bootstrap merge initialization.
  - schema/evolution: default/merge rules moved into dedicated `configmerge` section with `ConfigMerger` abstraction and tests.
  - persistence: dedicated writer service for in-memory config snapshots and atomic file persistence.
  - update surface: typed patch application moved into dedicated updater module with small option structs and validation.
- I will also add a light `ConfigView` interface with a concrete `LiveConfigView` so callers can receive immutable read-only settings views rather than using `Conf()` directly where possible.
- This will reduce large cross-cutting functions in `config.go` and align with suggested composable module boundaries.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Protocol and StarTram each duplicate contract-compatibility type/constant shims, creating coupled boundary boilerplate across modules.

evidence: actions.go: `type ContractCompatibility = contracts.ContractCompatibility` and `CompatibilityStable`, `CompatibilityDeprecated`, `CompatibilityRemoved`, `CompatibilityBackwardSafe` constants are redefined as aliases.
errors.go: same alias type and same four compatibility constants are redefined again for StarTram-specific use.
detail.suggestion: Extract a shared protocol-contract boundary module (or dedicated compatibility façade) that owns contract compatibility constants and expose canonical types once, then consume from both modules; remove duplicated re-export blocks.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Contract lifecycle checks are split into separate module-specific implementations, increasing risk of drift in compatibility semantics.

evidence: actions.go computes deprecation/active state in local helpers (`isActionContractDeprecated`, `isActionContractActive`) against `ContractMetadata`.

errors.go has independent exported helpers (`IsAPIConnectionErrorContractActive`, `IsAPIConnectionErrorContractDeprecated`, `APIConnectionErrorContractForVersion`) that separately evaluate version gates.
The two modules each maintain contract descriptors/metadata logic (`uploadActionContracts` + `apiConnectionErrorContract`) without a single shared contract registry.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared contract descriptor registry and generic lifecycle evaluator used by both modules so activation/removal/deprecation semantics remain consistent and centrally testable.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Orchestration runtime contracts are god-style bags, not domain-focused seams.

evidence: `dockerRuntime` in `runtime_seams.go` groups 10+ unrelated collaborator groups (context, file, container, image, config, urbit, wireguard, netdata, minio, command, volume, timer), creating a monolithic seam for many call sites.
`newDockerRuntime` populates all groups up front, even though a consumer like wireguard or urbit code only needs a narrow subset.
`wireguard.go` and `urbit.go` both accept the same runtime aggregate and read only feature-specific fields, so unrelated coupling is paid for even on narrow paths.
detail.suggestion: Split orchestration seams by domain boundary: create separate small runtime contracts for container/image, filesystem/artifacts, wireguard, urbit startup, and config snapshots. Construct each module (wireguard, urbit, collectors, subsystem) with only the subset it needs, and only keep a composed root runtime when explicit orchestration requires it.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Most runtime adapters are thin pass-throughs that add call depth without behavior.

evidence: `runtimeContainerAdapter`, `runtimeUrbitAdapter`, `runtimeSnapshotAdapter`, `startupBootstrapAdapter`, `startupImageAdapter`, and `startupLoadAdapter` in `runtime_seams.go` delegate directly to package functions with no translation.
`workflowRuntime` in `operations_runtime.go` similarly stores function fields that mostly forward directly into package-level actions and only checks nil, which repeats the same seam pattern.
startup wiring (`startupRuntime` and `startBackgroundServicesRuntime`) carries many function callbacks primarily to pass-through behavior instead of expressing explicit contracts.
detail.suggestion: Remove adapter layers where they perform only delegation: replace with direct function fields on focused interfaces (or constructor params) and keep adapters only when behavior adaptation, policy, or translation is needed. This reduces chain depth and makes intent explicit at boundaries.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Transition dispatch and broadcast state mutation are duplicated through stacked wrappers.

evidence: `RectifyUrbit` and `RectifyUrbitWithContext` repeat near-identical switch arms for each `Startram` event type before calling `applyTransitionUpdate`.
`applyTransitionUpdate` just forwards into `broadcast.ApplyBroadcastTransition(true, transition)` without adding additional domain policy.

`broadcast/transitions.go` contains duplicated core flow between `ApplyBroadcastTransition` and `ApplyBroadcastMutation` (read state, mutate, `UpdateBroadcast`, optional notify) with only minor API surface differences.
detail.suggestion: Consolidate transition dispatch into a small registry-driven strategy and expose a single mutation pipeline helper so adding new transition kinds is data-driven instead of repeated switch branching and wrapper chaining.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Contract governance is duplicated across protocol/actions and startram packages, creating drift risk as compatibility policy evolves.

evidence: `goseg/protocol/actions/actions.go` and `goseg/startram/errors.go` both re-declare equivalent compatibility aliases/constants and build contract descriptors in parallel.
Each file independently implements helper-style compatibility checks (`IsUploadActionContract*`, `IsAPIConnectionErrorContract*`) without shared policy ownership.
README identifies both as canonical contract surfaces, implying cross-module boundary coupling if policy changes are applied inconsistently.
detail.suggestion: Create a shared contract-governance module (or explicit API in `protocol/contracts`) for version policy descriptors and lifecycle helpers, and consume it from both modules so compatibility semantics remain single-sourced.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::cont.summary: Contract/version edge behavior is not directly unit-tested in the listed boundary files, so compatibility regressions can slip past scripted checks.

evidence: Public helpers expose boundary behavior critical to compatibility (`IsUploadActionContractDeprecated`, `IsUploadActionContractActive`, `APIConnectionErrorContractForVersion`) but no immediate unit tests are defined in these modules to validate transition boundaries.
README documents CI gates for contract change verification, but does not show deterministic unit tests for these version predicate edge cases in the same batch scope.
Coverage of these boundary predicates is especially important because contract acceptance/compatibility decisions drive runtime and wire compatibility.
detail.suggestion: Add explicit unit test files for both `goseg/protocol/actions` and `goseg/startram` that assert compatibility predicate behavior across introduced/deprecated/removed/empty-version inputs and verify `UnsupportedActionError`/masked-error return semantics.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::r.summary: Runtime seam layers add abstraction depth but no business behavior, increasing cognitive and refactor cost.

evidence: `runtime_seams.go` defines multiple wrapper interfaces and adapter types whose methods mostly forward directly to package functions (`StartContainer`, `StopContainerByName`, `UrbitConf`, `LoadWireguard`, etc.) without enrichment.
`startramRuntimeDefaultContainerOps` and `startramRuntimeServiceOverrides` mirror the same forwarding pattern and are re-wrapped rather than consolidated.
`accesspoint.go` and `wifi.go` similarly expose extensive function-pointer-based injection for each dependency edge, again with minimal policy translation.
This creates a systemic seam pattern where dependency boundaries are verbose but not semantically meaningful.
detail.suggestion: Create a narrower, intentional runtime contract per subsystem that contains only behavior-meaningful calls (e.g., `ContainerControl`, `SnapshotOps`) and inject concrete collaborators directly; remove no-op adapter layers and replace one-line wrappers with focused facades where behavior actually changes. This should reduce call depth and make seam tests assert intent, not plumbing.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::r.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::m.summary: Main bootstrap mixes startup orchestration, transport lifecycle, connectivity policy, and host-mode side effects in one flow.

evidence: `main.go` combines CLI parsing (`parseStartupOptions`), bootstrap wiring (`bootstrapSubsystems`), HTTP/WS server lifecycle (`startServerWithRuntime`), network recovery policy (`C2cCheckWith`), and kill-switch reboot timing (`killSwitch`) in a single execution path.

`startServerWithRuntime` simultaneously starts both listeners, owns shutdown orchestration, and does cross-server error handling, so changes to one transport concern affect unrelated startup and kill-switch paths.
Tests in `main_test.go` only exercise that callbacks are wired, not that these lifecycle responsibilities are separated into independently testable units.
detail.suggestion: Split startup into explicit collaborators (e.g., `StartupCoordinator`, `ServerLifecycleManager`, `ConnectivitySupervisor`) with dedicated interfaces and data structs; keep `main.go` as composition-only. Move kill-switch logic into a small policy service driven by connectivity snapshots, and keep transport boot logic transport-specific.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::m.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::s.summary: Client connection state transitions keep inactive placeholders and skip normalization, producing mixed semantics in auth-state transitions.

evidence: `transitionClientStateLocked` has a fast-path for `targetAuth && !client.Active` that appends a client and returns without setting active state, creating a branch with different semantics from the normal transition path.
`enqueueFakeConnIfNilOrUnconnected` explicitly inserts placeholder `MuConn` values, and cleanup/transition logic then has to special-case inactive/nil connections, increasing state-machine ambiguity.
The result is a brittle mix of transport lifecycle, placeholder management, and auth-state transitions in the same function, which is hard to reason about and extend safely.
detail.suggestion: Refactor `ClientManager` state updates around a single explicit transition function that always normalizes `(connection, active, bucket)` as an invariant and remove placeholder insertion from auth-state transitions. Represent temporary pending sessions separately from active session buckets to keep transition logic coherent.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::s.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Imported pier post-processing ignores ACME fix failures and continues as success.

evidence: In finalizeImportedPierReadiness, when ctx.Fix is true, acme.Fix errors are only logged and not propagated (lines 960-963), so callers still get nil and mark readiness as successful.
The same function later returns wrapped errors on wireguard switch failures, indicating this omission is inconsistent within one function's error contract.
detail.suggestion: Propagate acme.Fix failure as a wrapped error (or classify it as a typed soft-fail), and explicitly choose whether import should abort or continue; add a test that asserts the function fails when acme.Fix returns an error in finalized import path.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Import environment cleanup errors are logged but not surfaced, masking real setup failures.

evidence: prepareImportedPierEnvironment swallows DeleteContainer/DeleteVolume errors and continues to CreateVolume/MkdirAll (987-997), so stale container/volume state can persist or be silently bypassed.
This block is in an error-returning setup function where adjacent calls (CreateUrbitConfig, AppendSysConfigPier, CreateVolume, os.MkdirAll) use explicit `return fmt.Errorf(...)` semantics.
detail.suggestion: Replace blind log-and-continue with explicit handling: propagate unexpected failures, explicitly allow and wrap expected no-op cases (e.g., already absent), and add regression tests for deletion failures with different sentinel errors.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: HTTP/websocket startup can hide a failed listener behind a graceful shutdown from the other listener.

evidence: startServerWithRuntime returns nil on listener.ErrServerClosed, but only after the first listener event is received (359-366), without verifying no prior/future unexpected listener error occurred.
The tests assert listener failure is surfaced, but do not cover the reverse-order race where one listener exits ErrServerClosed while the other exits with non-nil error.
detail.suggestion: Track listener outcomes and only return nil when both listeners have exited with graceful shutdown (or context cancellation), otherwise return the first non-nil unexpected error; add a test that simulates one listener returning ErrServerClosed and the other returning a real error.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Session boundary is shared via package-level mutable state across auth modules, coupling behavior to process-wide globals.

evidence: `authsession/session.go` uses package-global auth/session boundary state and exposes mutators, and auth modules read those globals as implicit dependencies.
No explicit boundary/session object is threaded into call chains; cross-module behavior changes when one package sets shared state.
detail.suggestion: Refactor to pass an explicit session-boundary/state service into auth entrypoints (struct field or context), remove package mutators, and guard shared mutable maps with scoped synchronization if process-shared caches are required.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Nil request handling is inconsistent in token/session creation flow, creating avoidable panic paths.

evidence: `CreateToken` dereferences `r.Header` without a nil check in the request identity path.
Adjacent parsing helpers in the same package provide nil-safe behavior, so callers and tests can exercise mixed semantics unexpectedly.
detail.suggestion: Normalize request preprocessing in `tokens.go`: either require non-nil by contract with explicit early error at all callsites, or accept nil safely in one shared helper used by all token functions.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Container image/registry selection leaks special-case logic that bypasses the shared orchestration abstraction.

evidence: `registry.go` applies a hard-coded branch for `llama-api` that does not flow through the generic container-type resolution path.
Orchestration and orchestration consumers still dispatch behavior by raw string type matching, making each new container class require touching multiple switch points.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a typed container strategy map (e.g., interface/registry metadata struct with image resolver function) and remove one-off type conditionals in favor of declarative registrations.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::GS-T.summary: High-risk modules in this batch lack direct tests for concurrency, command construction, and branch-specific container resolution.

evidence: No tests are present for accesspoint command-building and execution boundary conditions in the listed package set.
Auth-session/global-boundary and persistence paths are mutation-heavy but lack deterministic unit tests for concurrent callers, nil guards, and rollback behavior.
detail.suggestion: Add focused tests around shell command assembly and execution boundaries, session boundary mutation races, and registry-special-case resolution paths using deterministic fakes and failure injection.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::G.summary: Access-point command execution uses shell-string formatting, reducing cohesion and observability of side-effect boundaries.

evidence: Command strings are composed from runtime values and executed via `sh -c`, coupling parsing, escaping, and execution into one brittle unit.
Error paths are emitted as low-level shell failures rather than structured command-domain errors, making fault attribution and recovery harder.
detail.suggestion: Build commands with typed argument arrays and call `exec.Command` directly; keep shelling only at a single adapter layer and return domain-specific errors with sanitized command metadata.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::G.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

subjective_review::goseg/auth/tokens/tok.summary: File changed since last review — re-review recommended

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Contract governance logic is duplicated across protocol/actions and startram, creating cross-module drift risk.

evidence: actions.go defines per-contract wrappers (`IsUploadActionContractActive`, `IsUploadActionContractDeprecated`, `IsC2CActionContractActive`, `IsC2CActionContractDeprecated`) for contract lifecycle checks.

startram/errors.go defines separate descriptor and lifecycle helpers (`APIConnectionErrorContractForVersion`, `IsAPIConnectionErrorContractActive`, `IsAPIConnectionErrorContractDeprecated`) for the same compatibility concern.
The duplicate patterns indicate contract-version policy is split instead of being owned by a single boundary.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared contracts governance API (in `groundseg/protocol/contracts`) that exposes contract registration and uniform active/deprecated checks, then have both packages consume that API instead of maintaining independent version-policy helpers and local metadata copies.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: ConfigView snapshot methods read live global runtime context, breaking snapshot isolation semantics.

evidence: `ConfigViewSnapshot` stores only `conf structs.SysConfig` but `RuntimeSettings()` and `CurrentRuntimeContext()` call `RuntimeContextSnapshot()` directly.
Methods intended to represent a snapshot (`NewConfigViewSnapshotFromConfig`) can return runtime fields that drift if global context changes after snapshot construction.
detail.suggestion: Store runtime context in the snapshot struct at construction time (for both `NewConfigViewSnapshot` and `NewConfigViewSnapshotFromConfig`) and have `RuntimeSettings()`/`CurrentRuntimeContext()` return those stored fields, so callers can rely on true snapshot immutability.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Several seam layers are thin function-bag wrappers that add wiring depth without policy or translation.

evidence: `importerRuntime` and `collectorRuntime` define many function fields and `default*Runtime()` merely assign concrete package functions.
`workflowRuntime` in `operations_runtime.go` and `WireguardRuntime`/`UrbitRuntime` projections in `runtime_seams.go` create another layer of pass-through indirection before actual call sites.

Callers mostly need a tiny subset of behaviors but must traverse shared projection structs and nil-check helpers (e.g., `dispatchUploadImportWithRuntime`, `copyWGFileToVolumeWithRuntime`) before behavior executes.
The packet reports high chain depths in this area, consistent with multi-layer indirection.
detail.suggestion: Replace the broad function-bag seam approach with narrower interfaces per subsystem (e.g., importer, collectors, ship workflow, docker orchestration) and provide concrete adapters only where call-time policy is needed; remove redundant projection functions in favor of direct dependency construction in package boundaries.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Contract governance is duplicated across protocol/actions and startram instead of shared policy.

evidence: `goseg/protocol/actions/actions.go` defines upload/c2c contract metadata plus active/deprecated helpers in-package.
`goseg/startram/errors.go` independently defines `APIConnectionErrorContractDescriptor` and per-version contract helpers.
The review packet explicitly flags duplicated contract-compatibility shims across these same packages, indicating an unresolved boundary pattern.
detail.suggestion: Create a single protocol-level contract registry (or shared contract-policy module) that owns version/compatibility metadata and policy checks, then have `goseg/protocol/actions/actions.go` and `goseg/startram/errors.go` consume that registry via narrow adapters so compatibility decisions stay consistent and centrally audited.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::l.summary: Workflow steps in startram toggle use a shared payload variable inside loop-constructed closures, so multiple steps can dispatch the same (last) payload.

evidence: `HandleStartramToggle` builds `workflow.Step` entries inside `for _, patp := range settings.Piers`.
It sets `payload := structs.WsUrbitPayload{...}` and captures `payload` directly in `Run: func() error { return startramDispatchUrbitPayloadFn(payload) }` without creating a per-iteration copy.
No intermediate function boundary enforces immutability of the per-pier action arguments before the closure executes.
detail.suggestion: Construct the payload inside each closure from a per-iteration copied ship id (for example, `patpCopy := patp`) or pass a closure that takes `patpCopy` explicitly, ensuring each step captures its own input value.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::p.summary: Runtime seam layers repeatedly use pass-through wrappers and thin duplicate types, creating low-leverage abstractions that obscure orchestration intent across modules.

evidence: `goseg/docker/orchestration/volume_writer.go` introduces `buildCopyFileToVolumeFn` that only delegates to `requiredCopyFileToVolumeFn` without policy or translation.
`volume_writer.go` aliases `writeConfigArtifactOptions = artifactwriter.WriteConfig` and wraps only to forward the call.
`container/helpers.go` defines a parallel `writeConfigArtifactOptions` type and forwarding wrapper performing only shallow field mapping.

`runtime_seams.go` repeatedly defines multiple function fields and option structs with repetitive nil-guard assignment logic for every operation group, while other seam packages mirror this same pattern.
detail.suggestion: Consolidate these seam layers into a small shared constructor/runtime helper with explicit policy hooks (e.g., one typed seam record and one normalization strategy), replacing repeated identity wrappers and copy-based option copying with a single coherent abstraction.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::p.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

subjective_review::goseg/docker/orchestr.summary: File changed since last review — re-review recommended

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Oversized callback-bag runtimes create high indirection and fragile wiring surfaces.

evidence: startupRuntime.withDefaults manually copies many individual function fields and startupRuntime.validate repeats the same callback inventory, indicating seam surface bloat.

startBackgroundServicesRuntime.validate enumerates a long list of required callbacks before any behavior runs, signaling orchestration defined by callback completeness rather than cohesive collaborators.
docker/orchestration runtime_seams defines many nested ops structs with dozens of function pointers and manual assignment in newDockerRuntime, increasing mapping overhead and drift risk.
importerRuntime uses multiple callback fields with explicit ensure* checks; this repeats the same callback-bag pattern in another subsystem.
detail.suggestion: Replace large function-pointer bags with smaller capability interfaces per use case (for example StartupInit, BackgroundServiceLauncher, ContainerImageResolver), then inject concrete structs that own related behavior. Keep constructor-time validation per capability interface instead of field-by-field nil checks.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Injected runtime seams are bypassed by direct global config/state reads in core paths.

evidence: ConstructPierInfoWithRuntime accepts collectorRuntime but still calls config.GetStartramConfig directly for backups/subdomains, so caller-provided runtime cannot fully control behavior.
areSubdomainsAliasesWithRuntime accepts workflowRuntime for dependency injection but directly calls config.GetStartramConfig for CNAME short-circuit logic.
startramTransitionCommand.Apply directly reads config.Conf while transition update flow otherwise uses command abstractions, mixing seam-based and global access patterns.
detail.suggestion: Make seam-bearing functions fully seam-owned: add snapshot/config providers to the runtime objects (or pass explicit immutable settings structs) and remove direct package-global config reads from "WithRuntime" code paths.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Multiple exported wrappers add call depth without adding policy or translation.

evidence: bootstrapRuntimeFns methods in main.go mostly perform nil checks and delegate directly to stored callbacks, creating an extra abstraction layer with little domain behavior.
bootstrapSubsystemsWithRuntime wraps runtime.ops methods into StartupOptions closures that simply forward calls, increasing indirection around startup flow.

docker/lifecycle exposes package-level functions (e.g., GetShipStatus, GetContainerImageTag, StartContainer, CreateContainer) that immediately delegate to defaultRuntime methods with no policy transformation.
subsystem entrypoints like StartDockerSubsystem call StartDockerSubsystemWithContext then swallow returned error, reducing usefulness of wrapper boundaries.
detail.suggestion: Collapse pass-through wrappers where no policy is added. Keep one public boundary per workflow (either function API or runtime object API), and move nil-check/defaulting into constructors so call sites invoke behavior directly.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Context and non-context API pairs duplicate logic instead of sharing a single core loop.

evidence: RectifyUrbit and RectifyUrbitWithContext contain nearly identical event dispatch switch logic, duplicated across two loops.
SmartDiskCheck and SmartDiskCheckWithContext duplicate body logic for listing/checking disks and notifications; only the wait/cancel mechanism differs.
DiskUsageWarning and DiskUsageWarningWithContext duplicate large switch-based warning update logic; only sleep vs select differs.
Similar dual-path patterns appear in routines/system (Start* and Start*WithContext variants), reinforcing repeated maintenance surfaces.
detail.suggestion: Refactor each pair to a single internal implementation that takes a wait/cancel strategy (or context + ticker abstraction). Keep one behavior core and expose thin adapters for legacy signatures.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Large callback-bag runtimes create high indirection cost and duplicated seam plumbing.

evidence: `runtime_seams.go` defines multiple wide structs of function fields plus many `With*` option-mergers that manually copy non-nil callbacks.
`startup_orchestrator.go` has broad runtime structs and a long `withDefaults`/`validate` sequence that repeats dependency wiring logic.
`operations_runtime.go` repeats this seam style (`workflowRuntime`) with nil-guard errors for missing callbacks, shifting correctness checks to runtime instead of constructor-time.
detail.suggestion: Replace callback bags with narrower capability interfaces per workflow boundary (e.g., lifecycle, transition publish, config read/write), and construct them with explicit required dependencies so missing wiring fails at compile-time or constructor-time. Keep optional hooks in small, local option structs instead of package-wide mega-runtimes.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Dual API pattern (`X` + `XWithRuntime`) adds wrapper depth without consistent leverage.

evidence: `collectors.go` repeatedly exposes thin wrappers that call `...WithRuntime(defaultCollectorRuntime(), ...)`.
`importer.go` and `operations_runtime.go` follow the same pattern for many entrypoints, creating two near-identical call paths per behavior.
`main.go` has parallel default-runtime wrappers (`bootstrapSubsystems`/`bootstrapSubsystemsWithRuntime`, `startServer`/`startServerWithRuntime`) with minimal policy differences.
detail.suggestion: Standardize on one public API shape: keep production entrypoints simple and move test seams to unexported constructors or package-level dependency injection points. Reduce exported wrapper pairs to only places where runtime substitution is meaningful at module boundaries.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Config snapshot abstraction is not consistently used, so callers bypass intended boundary.

evidence: `config_view.go` introduces `ConfigView`/snapshot methods intended to avoid direct mutable global reads.
`rectify.go` and `disk.go` still call `config.Conf()` directly in transition/loop logic rather than consuming a snapshot view dependency.
`runtime_seams.go` continues exposing direct config functions (`ConfFn`, `RuntimeContextSnapshot` closures), so consumers can bypass the view contract.
detail.suggestion: Enforce a single read abstraction for orchestration and long-running loops: pass `ConfigView` snapshots (or narrow settings snapshots) into workflows and remove direct `config.Conf()` access from non-config packages. Keep writes behind explicit mutation ports.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Routines facade duplicates logstream API as pass-through wrappers with no abstraction value.

evidence: `routines/logs.go` functions are mostly one-line forwarding wrappers to `logstream` plus repeated nil-context normalization.
`startup_orchestrator.go` uses these wrapper names as subsystem hooks, but the wrappers do not add policy, translation, or lifecycle guarantees beyond delegation.
Both packages now carry parallel API surfaces (`X` and `XWithContext`) increasing maintenance surface for the same behavior.
detail.suggestion: Either remove the facade and depend directly on `logstream` at call sites, or give the facade a real contract (policy, metrics, supervision behavior) and collapse duplicate function variants behind a single context-first API.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::s.summary: Transition lifecycle behavior is duplicated across ship and startram workflows instead of centralized in one orchestration model.

evidence: `urbit_operations.go` repeatedly emits transition states inline (for example `pack`, `packMeld`, `deleteShip`, `togglePower`, `rollChop`) with per-function start/error/success/clear handling.
`startram.go` separately re-implements transition sequencing via direct `startram.PublishEvent(...)` calls and `PublishTransitionWithPolicy(...)` wrappers for restart/toggle/register/endpoint flows.
`rectify.go` contains another distinct transition-application path (`applyTransitionUpdate`, `set*Transition` functions) that updates broadcast state independently from workflow emitters.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared transition orchestration component (typed state progression + terminal policy + clear timing) used by both shipworkflow and rectify paths; migrate existing operations to declarative transition plans instead of inline event strings.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.summary: Support and logstream modules duplicate the same log filename parsing/sorting structure.

evidence: Both files define a `LogFile` struct and parse `*.log` names by splitting on `-part-` (`support.go` and `logs.go`).
Both implementations sort by date and part number with nearly identical `sort.Slice` comparators.
This logic diverges only in retention count/consumer behavior, not parsing strategy, which indicates reusable shared logic was not extracted.
detail.suggestion: Extract log filename parsing and recency ordering into a shared package (for example `logger/logfiles`) and have support/reporting and cleanup routines call the same API with different retention policies.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::w.summary: Wi-Fi/C2C flow mixes infrastructure control, policy sequencing, and API surface across overlapping modules.

evidence: `wifi.go` combines loop/state management (`wifiInfoLoop`), system service control (`systemctl` enable/start/stop), network connect/reboot flows (`C2CConnect`), and API endpoints (`CaptiveAPI`, `CaptivePortal`) in one module.
`accesspoint.go` combines runtime normalization, dependency checks, process probing/matcher internals, and shell execution in the same package.
Cross-calls (`wifi.go` driving AP lifecycle while also owning C2C policy) indicate orchestration and adapter boundaries are not cleanly separated.
detail.suggestion: Split into explicit layers: Wi-Fi policy service (C2C state machine), AP adapter (hostapd/dnsmasq lifecycle), and HTTP/captive adapter; keep shell/process concerns behind interfaces and move sequencing rules into one coordinator.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Large callback-bag runtimes create high indirection cost without clear domain boundaries.

evidence: `runtime_seams.go` composes many function-pointer structs and option mutators (`WithContainerOps`, `WithUrbitOps`, `WithSnapshotOps`, etc.) that mostly perform nil checks and assignment.
`startup_orchestrator.go` `startupRuntime` and `withDefaults` repeat the same field-by-field callback override pattern across many members.
`operations_runtime.go` repeats the callback-bag pattern (`workflowRuntime`) with defensive nil checks and sentinel errors for missing callbacks.
Tests in `startup_orchestrator_test.go` include substantial configuration of callback bags, signaling high setup burden from abstraction shape.
detail.suggestion: Replace callback bags with smaller, role-focused interfaces (for example bootstrapping, transition emit, container lifecycle) and require dependencies via constructors so missing behavior fails at compile time instead of runtime nil checks.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Package-level forwarders duplicate APIs around singleton runtimes and add low-leverage abstraction layers.

evidence: `lifecycle.go` defines many package functions (`Initialize`, `GetShipStatus`, `StartContainer`, `DeleteContainer`, etc.) that simply call `defaultRuntime` methods.
`operations_runtime.go` public helpers repeatedly call `defaultWorkflowRuntime()` and then delegate into internal `...WithRuntime` variants, creating parallel entry paths.
`main.go` adds additional runtime wrapping (`bootstrapRuntime`, `bootstrapRuntimeFns`) that mirrors this forwarding style.
detail.suggestion: Standardize on one boundary style per subsystem: either explicit runtime instances passed from composition roots or direct package functions; remove redundant dual surfaces where wrappers do not enforce extra policy.
detail.investigation:
Investigated #1: moved config event handling into explicit startable loop and removed implicit launch from config.Initialize. Added StartConfEventLoop(ctx, <-chan string) with cancel semantics, kept legacy ConfChannel wrapper. Started loop from startup runtime using context. Added tests for event processing/cancelation and fixed auth-session fake-client handling to keep token-id auth behavior for nil websocket connections.

Investigating issue 1: remaining singleton forwarder duplication remains in shipworkflow and startup wiring.
- In shipworkflow/operations_runtime.go, exported transition/lookup helpers were delegating to runtime-specific ...WithRuntime variants without adding policy.
- In main.go, bootstrap/runtime struct still contains trivial startServer forwarding methods and function closures.
Next fix: collapse trivial wrapper layers by inlining singleton selection at the public seam and removing duplicate WithRuntime helper entry points where possible.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: The typed config view abstraction is not consistently used, so modules bypass it and couple directly to the global config bag.

evidence: `config_view.go` defines `ConfigView` and snapshot methods intended to expose narrower typed slices of config state.
`rectify.go` directly reads and mutates through `config.Conf`, `config.GetContainerState`, and `config.UpdateConfTyped` inside transition handlers.
`disk.go` loops fetch full `config.Conf()` and then read/write warning and pier data directly from `SysConfig` shape.
`urbit_operations.go` repeatedly calls `config.UrbitConf`/`config.GetContainerState` inline across workflow operations instead of using a narrowed view abstraction.
`structs/configs.go` `SysConfig` is a very wide aggregate, increasing accidental coupling when used as a general-purpose dependency.
detail.suggestion: Enforce `ConfigView` (or smaller purpose-built readers/writers) at orchestration boundaries, and phase out direct `config.Conf`/`config.UrbitConf` access in workflow and routine packages to reduce global bag coupling.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Parallel context/non-context APIs create low-leverage wrapper surface and duplicated orchestration.

evidence: `goseg/routines/logs.go` exports pairs like `SysLogStreamer` and `SysLogStreamerWithContext` where one is only a pass-through to the other package-level function after nil-context normalization.

`goseg/routines/system/system.go` maintains both non-context infinite-loop starters and context-aware loop variants for the same responsibilities, splitting lifecycle semantics across duplicate entrypoints.
`goseg/system/wifi.go` similarly exposes wrapper-style start helpers (`InitializeWiFi`, `StartWiFiInfoLoop`, private `startWiFiInfoLoop`) that layer lifecycle concerns over nearly identical behavior.
detail.suggestion: Adopt a single context-first public contract for long-running routines, keep only thin compatibility shims marked deprecated at package boundaries, and consolidate nil-context normalization in one helper to eliminate duplicated orchestration paths.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Wide callback-bag runtimes hide domain boundaries and force repetitive wiring code.

evidence: `startupRuntime` and `startBackgroundServicesRuntime` in `goseg/startup_orchestrator.go` contain many function fields and require large mirrored `withDefaults` and `validate` blocks that manually enumerate each callback.

Tests such as `goseg/startup_orchestrator_test.go` and `goseg/docker/orchestration/runtime_seams_test.go` build large stubs to satisfy broad runtime bags, indicating abstraction granularity is too coarse for focused behavior testing.

`goseg/main.go` introduces additional runtime bags (`bootstrapRuntime`, `serverRuntime`, `c2cRuntime`, `connectivityCheckRuntime`), extending the same pattern instead of narrowing seams around cohesive responsibilities.
detail.suggestion: Split runtime bags into smaller role-specific interfaces/structs (e.g., startup init, background services, network checks, container ops), require mandatory dependencies through constructors, and move optional behavior to narrowly scoped strategy objects.
detail.investigation:
## Issue 1 Analysis: dc3_volume_bootstrap_pattern_split

- Investigated orchestration volume bootstrapping paths in `netdata.go`, `wireguard.go`, `minio.go`, and `llama.go`.
- Implemented a shared bootstrap primitive in `volume_writer.go`:
  - Added `VolumeInitializationPlan` with explicit volume creation semantics.
  - Centralized copy/write fallback flow in `writeConfigArtifact`.
  - Made volume existence/creation conditional and shared with copy fallback.
- Wired runtime-specific plan configuration through existing `dockerRuntime` seam:
  - `netdata`, `wireguard`, and `minio` now use runtime-provided volume operations for bootstrapping.
  - Llama now uses the same plan constructor via its existing helper.
- Updated tests to use deterministic no-op volume ops in runtime seams where Docker is not available.

## Issue 2 Analysis: 8bd44875

- Reviewed `backup_runtime.go` and identified transport, crypto, and restore-runtime responsibilities coexisting in one file.
- Split runtime behavior into focused files:
  - `startram/backup_transport.go` now owns API call and download verification logic.
  - `startram/backup_crypto.go` now owns AES-GCM encrypt/decrypt helpers.
  - `startram/backup_restore_runtime.go` now owns restore request types and restore-runtime orchestration/volume replay flow.
- Kept orchestration boundary in `backup_service.go` unchanged (interface + orchestration entrypoints) and retained existing public functions by moving implementations out of `backup_runtime.go`.
- Added compatibility coordinator in `backup_runtime.go` to document the new split and avoid a monolithic mixed-concern module.
## Issue 8f Issue 1 Analysis: desk_lifecycle_duplication

- Investigated `goseg/shipworkflow/urbit_operations.go` where install/remove desk handlers for `penpai` and `groundseg` duplicated status/action/wait orchestration.
- The shared flow is: read desk state, branch install/revive/wait for install path, uninstall, then wait for expected running state change.
- Confirmed this flow is currently wrapped only by `runDeskTransition` and duplicated across 4 functions.
- I will replace this with a generic desk lifecycle helper that owns the shared transition flow and accepts per-desk parameters (desk name and operation callbacks), reducing duplicate code and adding a central place for action messages.
## Issue 2 Analysis: routines-capability-blend

- Investigated `routines/disk.go` and `routines/logs.go` against the current finding and confirmed mixed domain responsibilities (disk health + log transport/streaming).
- Implemented domain split under `routines/healthcheck` and `routines/logstream`:
  - Moved existing disk polling/state logic and notification helpers into `routines/healthcheck`.
  - Moved legacy log cleanup/splitting, websocket streaming, and session cleanup logic into `routines/logstream`.
- Replaced `routines` root `disk.go`/`logs.go` with thin delegation functions to preserve exported API used by startup orchestration.
- Relocated corresponding tests to their new packages and kept behavior-equivalent coverage.
## Issue 3 Analysis: bcast_state_loss_on_collector_error

- Investigated `runBroadcastTickWithRuntime` and confirmed the pier collector error path currently allowed `pierInfo` to become empty, which then overwrote in-memory urbit state for that tick.
- Changed failure behavior in `broadcast/loop.go` to retain the last-known `broadcastState.Urbits` when `constructPierInfoFn` returns an error.
- Added regression coverage in `broadcast/loop_test.go` to assert `Transition` and `Info` for existing urbits are preserved across a simulated collector failure.

## Issue 4 Analysis: startup_optional_runtime_handoffs

- Investigated startup runtime handoff in `goseg/startup_orchestrator.go`.
- Introduced explicit runtime contract validation:
  - Added required-callback metadata and validation checks for `startupRuntime`.
  - Added required callback checks for `startBackgroundServicesRuntime`, with `syncRetrieve` conditionally required when `startramWgRegistered == true`.
- Updated bootstrap path:
  - `Bootstrap` now validates the merged startup runtime before task execution.
  - `startBackgroundServicesWithRuntime` now validates background callbacks and returns an error on missing required hooks.
  - Required background callbacks are invoked without silent nil-guarding after validation.
- Added/updated unit tests in `goseg/startup_orchestrator_test.go` to cover:
  - missing startup runtime callbacks rejection,
  - missing background runtime callbacks rejection,
  - expected callback startup sequencing when required hooks are supplied,
  - no-op behavior when startram sync is intentionally disabled.

## Issue 5 Analysis: urbit_conf_snapshot_stale_mutation

- Investigated `urbitContainerConfWithRuntime` in `goseg/docker/orchestration/urbit.go`.
- Fixed stale in-memory config usage by refreshing effective ship config after `UpdateUrbit` writes.
- Switched subsequent container decision logic (`BootStatus`, script path, ports, runtime settings, network mode, mounts) to the reloaded config.
- Added regression coverage in `goseg/docker/orchestration/urbit_test.go` to ensure a config mutation that changes `BootStatus` is honored immediately and drives expected script selection.

## Issue 6 Analysis: c2c_killswitch_no_shutdown_boundary

- Investigated C2C check and lifecycle wiring in `goseg/main.go`.
- Changed C2C check entrypoints to accept a context so kill-switch startup can observe shutdown signals.
- Updated kill-switch loop (`killSwitch`) to accept the same context, use timer+select and return when context is canceled.
- Threaded context through startup callback flow (`StartupOptions.StartC2cCheck`, startup orchestrator background startup path, and main bootstrap wiring) so C2C maintenance stops with application shutdown.
- Added regression test in `goseg/main_test.go` verifying `killSwitch` exits promptly when context is done.
- Evidence confirms config.go and version.go still combine configuration loading, mutation patch construction, runtime snapshots, merge logic, and utility helpers.
- I will split boundaries into focused modules while preserving exported API for callers:
  - loader/parsing: reading raw system.json, default config creation, bootstrap merge initialization.
  - schema/evolution: default/merge rules moved into dedicated `configmerge` section with `ConfigMerger` abstraction and tests.
  - persistence: dedicated writer service for in-memory config snapshots and atomic file persistence.
  - update surface: typed patch application moved into dedicated updater module with small option structs and validation.
- I will also add a light `ConfigView` interface with a concrete `LiveConfigView` so callers can receive immutable read-only settings views rather than using `Conf()` directly where possible.
- This will reduce large cross-cutting functions in `config.go` and align with suggested composable module boundaries.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Repeated default/withRuntime/internal call chains add indirection with limited semantic gain.

evidence: `goseg/docker/orchestration/wireguard.go` repeatedly layers exported functions into `*WithRuntime` into private helpers (`WriteWgConf` -> `WriteWgConfWithRuntime` -> `writeWgConfWithRuntime`, similar patterns for load/build/copy), even where middle layers add no policy.

`goseg/shipworkflow/operations_runtime.go` has many default-wrapper pairs (`runUrbitTransition`/`runUrbitTransitionWithRuntime`, `WaitComplete`/`waitCompleteWithRuntime`, alias helpers) plus runtime default-merging, increasing call depth and cognitive load.

`goseg/broadcast/collectors/collectors.go` and `goseg/importer/importer.go` follow the same pattern (`ConstructPierInfo` -> `construct...WithRuntime`; `Initialize` -> `initializeWithRuntime`) across many operations, suggesting systemic over-extraction.
detail.suggestion: Collapse to one explicit dependency boundary per subsystem: keep internal functions dependency-parameterized, expose a minimal public API that injects defaults once, and remove intermediate pass-through `WithRuntime` layers that do not enforce policy or translation.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::w.summary: Transition workflows are implemented as repeated ad-hoc step assembly across modules, indicating missing shared orchestration abstraction.

evidence: urbit_operations repeatedly builds near-identical transition plans (`EmitStart`, `ErrorEvent`, `ClearDelay`) around bespoke step lists for many actions.
startram handlers build similarly structured multi-step workflows inline (`HandleStartramToggle`, `HandleStartramRegister`, `HandleStartramEndpoint`) with the same lifecycle concerns.
rectify handlers duplicate event-loop and transition-apply scaffolding for urbit/new ship/system/startram streams.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a domain-level workflow DSL or template helpers (pre/post hooks, standard events, retry/publish policy) shared by urbit/startram/rectify transitions; keep each action handler focused on business rules rather than lifecycle plumbing.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::u.summary: The urbit operations module mixes multiple business domains and operational concerns in one large file, reducing cohesion and increasing coupling.

evidence: urbit_operations contains desk lifecycle logic, startram service cleanup, container lifecycle toggles, config mutations, export logic, and scheduling logic in the same module.
The file directly coordinates click commands, docker state updates, config persistence, and broadcast side effects, which are distinct responsibilities.
docker subsystem recovery and startram flows consume overlapping lifecycle behaviors, reinforcing cross-module coupling to this monolithic operations layer.
detail.suggestion: Split `urbit_operations` into focused services (e.g., `desk_service`, `maintenance_service`, `network_service`, `export_service`) behind a narrow facade so handlers compose capabilities instead of embedding all concerns.
detail.investigation: Refactored urbit operations responsibilities into focused files by area: desk lifecycle, runtime/container operations, pack/roll/chop lifecycle, export/cleanup, and startram service ops. Added explicit adapter facade () exposing a narrow public API while moving implementation into domain-specific files to reduce cross-concern coupling and file cohesion risk.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::g.summary: Package-level mutable function seams and globals create hidden dependency direction and inconsistent composition boundaries.

evidence: rectify uses many package-level function vars (`getStartramSettings`, `updateConfTypedFn`, `getUrbitConfigFn`) as implicit dependencies.
startram uses package globals for dispatch/runtime builders (`startramRuntimeFn`, `startramDispatchUrbitPayloadFn`, `startramRecoverWireguardFleetFn`).
accesspoint and docker/network also rely on mutable package-level runtime hooks (`checkDependenciesFn`, `isRunningFn`, `dockerClientNew`, `networkOperationTimeout`).
detail.suggestion: Standardize on explicit constructor-injected runtime structs per subsystem (no mutable package globals), then pass dependencies through coordinator types to make module boundaries and ownership explicit.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::l.summary: Logging stream management is split across logger/session/logstream with duplicated control loops and overlapping responsibilities.

evidence: logstream implements both context and non-context versions for sys/docker stream loops with duplicated internals (`SysLogStreamer` vs `SysLogStreamerWithContext`, `DockerLogStreamer` vs context variant).
logger both configures zap and acts as a session facade (`SysLogSessions`, `SetDockerLogSession*`, `RemoveSysSessions`), while session/ws manages connection lifecycle separately.
Cross-package delegation makes ownership unclear (logger -> session wrappers, logstream -> logger session APIs), increasing indirection for simple stream-path changes.
detail.suggestion: Define a single log-stream service boundary that owns session registries and stream loops; keep `logger` focused on logging backend initialization/writers and move websocket/session orchestration into one dedicated runtime service.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::c.summary: Configuration patching combines schema definition, parsing, mutation, persistence, and public API construction in one module.

evidence: `config_update.go` defines `ConfPatch`, registry schema (`confPatchRegistry`), parsing helpers, storage update flow, and dozens of `WithX` option constructors together.
The same module maintains both typed option-path updates (`UpdateConfTyped`) and map-driven patch path (`UpdateConf`), expanding behavioral surface in one place.
Tests and adjacent config modules must account for this broad module behavior, increasing blast radius of changes.
detail.suggestion: Separate config concerns into modules: schema/field registry, patch parsing, persistence adapter, and option constructors; then expose a small orchestrator API that composes them.

- Introduced explicit runtime contract validation:
  - Added required-callback metadata and validation checks for `startupRuntime`.
  - Added required callback checks for `startBackgroundServicesRuntime`, with `syncRetrieve` conditionally required when `startramWgRegistered == true`.
- Updated bootstrap path:
  - `Bootstrap` now validates the merged startup runtime before task execution.
  - `startBackgroundServicesWithRuntime` now validates background callbacks and returns an error on missing required hooks.
  - Required background callbacks are invoked without silent nil-guarding after validation.
- Added/updated unit tests in `goseg/startup_orchestrator_test.go` to cover:
  - missing startup runtime callbacks rejection,
  - missing background runtime callbacks rejection,
  - expected callback startup sequencing when required hooks are supplied,
  - no-op behavior when startram sync is intentionally disabled.

## Issue 5 Analysis: urbit_conf_snapshot_stale_mutation

- Investigated `urbitContainerConfWithRuntime` in `goseg/docker/orchestration/urbit.go`.
- Fixed stale in-memory config usage by refreshing effective ship config after `UpdateUrbit` writes.
- Switched subsequent container decision logic (`BootStatus`, script path, ports, runtime settings, network mode, mounts) to the reloaded config.
- Added regression coverage in `goseg/docker/orchestration/urbit_test.go` to ensure a config mutation that changes `BootStatus` is honored immediately and drives expected script selection.

## Issue 6 Analysis: c2c_killswitch_no_shutdown_boundary

- Investigated C2C check and lifecycle wiring in `goseg/main.go`.
- Changed C2C check entrypoints to accept a context so kill-switch startup can observe shutdown signals.
- Updated kill-switch loop (`killSwitch`) to accept the same context, use timer+select and return when context is canceled.
- Threaded context through startup callback flow (`StartupOptions.StartC2cCheck`, startup orchestrator background startup path, and main bootstrap wiring) so C2C maintenance stops with application shutdown.
- Added regression test in `goseg/main_test.go` verifying `killSwitch` exits promptly when context is done.
- Evidence confirms config.go and version.go still combine configuration loading, mutation patch construction, runtime snapshots, merge logic, and utility helpers.
- I will split boundaries into focused modules while preserving exported API for callers:
  - loader/parsing: reading raw system.json, default config creation, bootstrap merge initialization.
  - schema/evolution: default/merge rules moved into dedicated `configmerge` section with `ConfigMerger` abstraction and tests.
  - persistence: dedicated writer service for in-memory config snapshots and atomic file persistence.
  - update surface: typed patch application moved into dedicated updater module with small option structs and validation.
- I will also add a light `ConfigView` interface with a concrete `LiveConfigView` so callers can receive immutable read-only settings views rather than using `Conf()` directly where possible.
- This will reduce large cross-cutting functions in `config.go` and align with suggested composable module boundaries.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Several background services suppress runtime failures instead of propagating them to startup supervision.

evidence: In `StartDockerSubsystemWithContext`, both goroutines always send `nil` to `errs` after function return (`docker.go` lines 52-59), while `dockerListenerWithContext` logs event-stream errors (`line 151`) and never returns an error to caller.

`startup_orchestrator.superviseBackgroundService` relies on function return values to mark failures (`startup_orchestrator.go` lines 417-419), so logged-but-not-returned failures remain invisible to health checks.
`SysLogStreamerWithContext` and `OldLogsCleanerWithContext` repeatedly `continue` on parse/filesystem errors (`logs.go` lines 66-68, 73-75, 134-135) without escalating repeated failure conditions.
detail.suggestion: Define a consistent policy for long-running service failures: return terminal errors to supervisor, and for recoverable errors track/backoff with thresholded escalation. Update Docker/logstream routines to emit structured errors instead of only logging.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Error context preservation is inconsistent due to mixed `%w`, `%v`, and raw returns at boundary functions.

evidence: `initializeWithRuntime` wraps storage-path failures with context but returns raw `err` for `os.MkdirAll` failures (`importer.go` lines 150-156 vs 167-171).
`checkDependencies` returns raw `exec.LookPath` errors (`accesspoint.go` lines 304-310), while nearby code wraps command failures with context (`lines 330-332`).
`GetDisk` returns raw errors on opening `/proc/mounts` and scanner errors (`system.go` lines 55-57, 113-114), but in-function `Statfs` failures are wrapped with mount context (`line 100`).
detail.suggestion: Standardize boundary behavior to always wrap outward-facing errors with operation context using `%w`; reserve raw returns for internal helpers only. Add lint/tests around wrapping in package entrypoints.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: WiFi command failures are collapsed into sentinel values, making infra errors indistinguishable from legitimate states.

evidence: `ifCheck` returns `false` on command execution failure after logging (`wifi.go` lines 134-138), conflating command failure with 'radio disabled'.
`getConnectedSSID` returns empty string on interface query failure and silently skips BSS errors (`wifi.go` lines 191-194, 198-200), again collapsing error states into normal absence.
`C2cCheckWith` branches on boolean connectivity/device signals (`main.go` lines 158-165), so sentinel collapse can trigger mode transitions based on tooling failure rather than real network state.
detail.suggestion: Change WiFi status probes to return `(value, error)` consistently and propagate into C2C decision logic; treat command/runtime failures separately from negative domain states.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Leak protocol handlers report some malformed inputs to clients but silently drop other processing/transport failures.

evidence: `handleAction` reports protocol decode failures via `reportLeakProtocolError` (`handler.go` lines 49-52) but only logs `processAction` failures without client feedback (`lines 54-56`).
`processAction` returns `nil` when ship status is missing (`handler.go` lines 116-119), dropping payloads without signaling either caller or channel.
In listener read loop, connection read errors close `readChan` and return with no contextual error propagation (`broadcast.go` lines 42-47), unlike other paths that accumulate/send errors.
detail.suggestion: Unify leak failure policy: classify client-reportable protocol errors vs transport/internal errors, and consistently emit either protocol error payloads or structured internal errors with metrics.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Cross-module error wrapping alternates between `%w` and `%v`, causing unpredictable loss of causal chains.

evidence: `goseg/system/disk.go:180` and `:184` return `fmt.Errorf(...: %v, err)` while nearby code uses `%w` (`:269`).
`goseg/shipworkflow/urbit_operations_runtime.go:148`, `:237`, `:402` use `%v` for wrapped operational errors while sibling paths in the same file use `%w` (`:320`, `:327`, `:334`).
`goseg/logger/logger.go:280`, `:293`, `:308` use `%v`, preventing reliable upstream `errors.Is/As` classification.
detail.suggestion: Standardize to `%w` whenever returning an existing error, reserve `%v` for logging only, and add a lint/check (or shared helper) to enforce wrapping consistency at return boundaries.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Error handling decisions depend on brittle substring matching instead of typed/sentinel errors.

evidence: `goseg/docker/lifecycle/lifecycle.go:449-453` treats any error containing "not found" as a missing container.
`goseg/importer/importer.go:1027-1035` ignores cleanup failures by scanning `err.Error()` for "not found"/"no such".

Siblings emit different untyped not-found messages (`goseg/docker/lifecycle/lifecycle.go:518` "Container not found" vs `goseg/shipworkflow/operations_runtime.go:328` "status doesn't exist"), making cross-package classification ad hoc.
detail.suggestion: Introduce shared sentinel/typed not-found errors at docker/import/workflow boundaries and replace message parsing with `errors.Is` checks, while mapping low-level provider errors once at boundary adapters.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Several operational write/restart failures are logged but not propagated, creating inconsistent success semantics.

evidence: `goseg/shipworkflow/urbit_operations_runtime.go:163-189` logs container start/stop failures and still returns nil from the transition step.
`goseg/routines/logstream/logs.go:390-393` file deletion failures are printed and ignored, and the function returns success.
`goseg/docker/orchestration/subsystem/docker.go:244-246` transition broadcast failures are downgraded to warn-only while other transition failures can trip hard failure thresholds (`:185-190`).
detail.suggestion: Define per-operation error policy (critical vs best-effort) and encode it explicitly via return types or wrapped policy errors; propagate non-best-effort failures consistently and aggregate best-effort failures into structured warnings.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: EOF handling in log splitting uses string equality and shadowed error variables, making boundary behavior fragile.

evidence: `goseg/routines/logstream/logs.go:333` checks `err.Error() != "EOF"` instead of `errors.Is(err, io.EOF)`.
In the same loop, `err` is reassigned by `chunkWriter.WriteString` (`:353`), so later EOF checks (`:359`) do not refer to the read error anymore.
Elsewhere in scope, error handling already expects wrapped/sentinel-style checks (`goseg/docker/lifecycle/lifecycle.go:449-453`), highlighting inconsistency in boundary error strategy.
detail.suggestion: Use separate `readErr`/`writeErr` variables, switch EOF logic to `errors.Is(readErr, io.EOF)`, and return wrapped context for non-EOF read/write failures.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::r.summary: Orchestration uses oversized function-bag runtimes with repetitive merge plumbing, creating high indirection and weak structural boundaries.

evidence: `runtime_seams.go` defines many broad bags (`Runtime`, `StartupRuntime`, `StartramRuntime`, `dockerRuntime*Ops`) and multiple `merge*` functions that manually copy each field (for example `mergeRuntimeContainerOps`, `mergeRuntimeUrbitOps`, `mergeRuntimeLoadOps`).
The same runtime-injection style appears again in other subsystems (`bootstrapRuntime` in `main.go`, `RectifyRuntime` in `rectify.go`), indicating a repeated pattern rather than a local necessity.
Most of these bags expose low-level function pointers directly instead of smaller capability-focused interfaces, so dependency shape grows with each feature.
detail.suggestion: Introduce narrower capability interfaces per workflow (for example `ContainerStarter`, `ShipConfigUpdater`, `TransitionPublisher`) and compose them in a small runtime root; replace hand-written per-field merge functions with constructor defaults plus explicit option groups.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::s.summary: Urbit operation handlers duplicate the same transition-template and mutate/restart sequence, increasing drift risk.

evidence: `setUrbitDomain`, `setMinIODomain`, `togglePower`, `toggleNetwork`, `handleLoom`, and `handleSnapTime` all repeat the same shape: build transition config, mutate config, optionally recycle container, optionally restart.
Handlers repeatedly inline similar event constants (`loading`, `done`, `error`, clear delay settings) and update patterns (`persistShipConf` + restart path), rather than sharing policy-level helpers.
`rectify.go` contains a parallel transition-reducer/event-router layer for similar domain events, so behavioral rules are split across two repetition-heavy paths.
detail.suggestion: Extract reusable operation archetypes (for example `mutateConfigOnly`, `mutateAndRecycleIfBooted`, `aliasUpdateWithValidation`) and centralize transition event presets to data-driven descriptors consumed by both workflow and rectify layers.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::e.summary: The process entrypoint combines web serving, connectivity policy, and device reboot behavior in one module.

evidence: `main.go` contains HTTP/websocket server lifecycle (`runServer`), startup parsing, C2C checks (`C2cCheckWith`), and a reboot kill-switch (`killSwitch`) that calls `subsystem.GracefulShipExit()` and executes `reboot`.
C2C and wifi state logic is also maintained in `system/wifi.go` (`SetC2CMode`, wifi loop state), creating cross-module policy coupling.

Docker shutdown semantics used by the kill-switch live in `docker/orchestration/subsystem/docker.go` (`GracefulShipExit`), so process-control behavior spans three modules without a dedicated orchestration boundary.
detail.suggestion: Split `main` into explicit composition roots: `server`, `connectivity-policy`, and `device-control`; define a single coordinator service for C2C mode and reboot policy to remove direct cross-module calls from the entrypoint.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::e.note: not reported in latest holistic re-import

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.summary: Container restart and state reconciliation logic is duplicated across lifecycle and orchestration subscription paths.

evidence: `lifecycle.go` implements restart/reconcile behavior (`ensureRunningContainer`, `recreateContainerIfImageChanged`, `ensureMonitoredContainerHealthy`) while `subsystem/docker.go` independently handles stop/die restart policy (`dockerDieAfterTransition`, `defaultRestartAfterDeath`, `defaultStopTransitionRestart`).
Both modules mutate and interpret `DesiredStatus`/`ActualStatus` semantics, but they do so in separate flows and timing models.
Because health recovery and event-driven recovery are separated, behavioral consistency depends on duplicated policy assumptions instead of one canonical state machine.
detail.suggestion: Define a single container-state policy engine (event + health inputs -> desired action) and have both lifecycle pollers and Docker event handlers delegate to it; keep one authoritative transition model for desired/actual state updates.
