review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Background/update and state-mutation paths mix fail-open and fail-fast handling without a shared policy, creating unpredictable failure modes.

evidence: `goseg/routines/version.go` (`callUpdater`, `updateBinary`) logs many operational failures and returns without surfacing an error to callers, so failed update stages are silent to orchestration.

`goseg/shipworkflow/urbit_operations_runtime.go:304-311` logs status-fetch errors in `toggleBootStatus` but still computes and persists `BootStatus`, allowing state changes after failed prerequisites.
`goseg/system/disk.go:304-306` explicitly downgrades multipart cleanup failure to warning in `SetupTmpDir`, while nearby setup errors in the same function are treated as fatal and returned.
`goseg/startup_orchestrator.go:70-100` and `597-649` enforce required/optional policy in some startup paths, but this policy is not reused by the version and ship workflow paths above.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared error-handling policy abstraction (for example: required/optional/best-effort with explicit typed outcomes) and require each operation to declare its policy. Route all major workflows through it so degradations are explicit (status/result object) rather than ad-hoc log-and-continue branches.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Background worker wrappers frequently discard returned errors, turning hard failures into log-only behavior with no propagation path.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.note: Return errors from non-context background wrappers in logstream; make StartDockerSubsystem return its WithContext error result and log/ring handlers consume returned transition loop errors.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Malformed system log payloads are silently dropped, creating non-deterministic behavior where log delivery failures are invisible.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.note: Emit structured warning on malformed system log payload compaction failures and include bounded sample metadata and occurrence count.

detail.suggestion: Replace broad ConfigView usage with narrow, feature-specific settings providers (for example SwapConfigProvider, HealthCheckConfigProvider) injected per subsystem; keep mutable global config behind explicit domain services and reduce package-level snapshot helpers.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Multiple long-running loops read channels without closure checks, so producer shutdown is not handled as an explicit failure mode.

evidence: `runTransitionEventLoop` uses `case event := <-ch` with no `ok` check; closed channels can repeatedly deliver zero values and bypass explicit termination/error reporting.

`dockerListenerWithContext` reads `event := <-messages` and `err := <-errs` without `ok`; when channels close, logic falls into nil/zero-value paths (`continue` on nil err) instead of deterministic shutdown error semantics.
detail.suggestion: Use two-value receives (`v, ok := <-ch`) in long-lived loops; treat `!ok` as a terminal boundary and either return a wrapped error or exit explicitly with a clear reason.

subjective_review::goseg/shipworkflow/ne.summary: File changed since last review — re-review recommended

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Function-field runtime bags are over-fragmented, creating high wiring/validation overhead without equivalent behavioral leverage.

evidence: `runtime_seams.go` defines many callback bags and option-merging layers, then startup has separate callback bags and validation paths in `startup_orchestrator.go`.
Tests in `runtime_seams_test.go` and `startup_orchestrator_test.go` require extensive stub structs and nil-guard scaffolding to assemble runtimes, indicating high indirection cost at call sites.
Most validators check callback presence rather than domain invariants, suggesting the abstraction is primarily wiring complexity.
detail.suggestion: Collapse callback-bag fragmentation into fewer behavior-oriented interfaces (or concrete dependency structs) per workflow boundary, with constructor-time defaults and invariant checks; reduce per-function callback exposure to only true variability points.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Several runtime projection layers are pass-through adapters that add call depth but little translation or policy.

evidence: `NewDockerTransitionRuntime` and `NewDockerHealthRuntime` in `runtime_seams.go` mainly copy fields from `Runtime` to narrower structs.
`docker.go` consumes projected runtimes but still directly invokes forwarded function pointers for core operations, so the projection layer contributes limited behavior.

`operations_runtime.go` has additional dispatch wrappers (`DispatchUploadImport` -> `DispatchUploadImportWithCoordinator` -> `dispatchUploadImport`) that mostly forward runtime function calls after defaulting.
detail.suggestion: Replace projection/copy layers with behavior-bearing adapters (methods implementing domain policies) or consume one cohesive runtime where narrowing does not add semantic value; remove wrapper hops that only forward calls.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: Seams frequently expose global config/state access instead of establishing explicit state boundaries.

evidence: `NewRectifyRuntime` wires straight to `config.*` globals (`GetContainerState`, `UrbitConf`, `UpdateConfTyped`, etc.), so runtime abstraction does not isolate mutable global state.

`importer.go` uses package-level function vars defaulting to config/global operations (`importerSwapSettingsFn`, `importerStartramConfigFn`, `updateContainerStateFn`), creating implicit coupling and hidden override points.

`main.go` default runtime builders also pull config globals directly (`config.BasePath`, `config.ConnectivitySettingsSnapshot`, `config.NetCheck`), extending config coupling across orchestration boundaries.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a single explicit process/runtime context object instantiated at bootstrap and passed through orchestrators; remove package-level function-var seams for config access and migrate to constructor-injected dependencies.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.note: Refactored main/importer/rectify seams to use explicit runtime context and dependency injection: Rectify now constructs via NewRectifyRuntime/defaultRectifyRuntimeDependencies and passes runtime into startram transition handlers; importer removed package-level config callback vars by moving swap/startram/container-state access into importerRuntime and runtime-accessor methods, with workflow functions threaded with runtime. main introduced c2cRuntimeContext and runtime constructors with explicit connectivity/debug callbacks, including killSwitchWithMode and explicit netcheck wiring.

resolution_attestation.text: I have actually introduced constructor-injected runtime/context seams and removed package-level config callback access for these flows, and I am not gaming the score by resolving without fixing.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: The `UrbitDocker` model is a monolithic cross-domain bag, weakening abstraction boundaries and encouraging broad mutable updates.

evidence: `structs.UrbitDocker` combines networking, scheduling, backup, operational flags, and UI-related toggles into one large struct with custom unmarshal logic.
`defaults/defaults.go` initializes this broad object as a single default unit, reinforcing its role as a global parameter bag.
Runtimes expose broad read/mutate hooks (`UrbitConfFn`, `UrbitConfAllFn`, `UpdateUrbitFn`) that allow many concerns to flow through one model rather than concern-specific interfaces.
detail.suggestion: Split `UrbitDocker` into bounded sub-structures (network/runtime/backup/feature flags) and provide targeted update APIs per concern to reduce incidental coupling and improve interface honesty.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::g.summary: Global mutable seam variables are used as the primary dependency boundary, creating hidden coupling and test-reset overhead across modules.

evidence: `runtime_seams.go` defines package-level function vars (e.g., `runtimeBasePathFn`, `runtimeDockerDirFn`) that are read by default runtime builders rather than injected per instance.
`wifi.go` exposes many package-level seam vars (`execCommandForWiFi`, `runCommandForWiFi`, `wifiNewClientForWiFi`) plus mutable global state (`Device`, `WifiInfo`, `c2cEnabled`).
`logger.go` similarly keeps mutable package-level init/runtime seam state (`sysLogSink`, `mkdirAllFn`, `pathResolverFn`, init-state globals).
Tests rely on manual seam reset helpers (`resetWifiSeamsForTest`, `resetLoggerInitForTest`), showing mutation-based DI is systemic and leaks across test boundaries.
detail.suggestion: Introduce explicit subsystem runtime objects owned per test/process (constructor-injected interfaces), and remove package-level mutable seam vars. Keep one immutable default wiring function for production and pass dependencies explicitly to call sites.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::d.summary: Docker lifecycle/subsystem logic is concentrated into broad modules that mix orchestration policy, state reconciliation, transport, and recovery behavior.

evidence: `lifecycle.go` (614 lines) contains container planning, image management, start/create/stop/delete/restart operations, exec command plumbing, polling loop health logic, and utility helpers.
`subsystem/docker.go` combines event listener I/O, subscription routing, transition workflow execution, failure-window policy, and restart scheduling logic in one file.
`volume_writer.go` contains low-level temp-container mechanics and image selection policy that also appears in higher-level runtime wiring.
detail.suggestion: Split into coherent units: image/plan resolver, container reconciler, docker command executor, and health/event supervisors. Move policy (restart windows, monitored container set) to dedicated policy modules and keep transport/client code in adapters.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::c.summary: Configuration update schema is duplicated between patch struct fields and registry mappings, increasing drift risk and maintenance cost.

evidence: `config_update_schema.go` is large (575 lines) with a wide `ConfPatch` struct plus a long `confPatchRegistry` that separately encodes parse/apply behavior per key.
Adding/changing a config key requires touching multiple disconnected loci (patch field, registry entry, parsing/apply closures, and tests), not a single source of truth.
`config_test.go` contains extensive merge/update behavior checks, indicating high coordination burden around schema evolution.
detail.suggestion: Adopt a declarative field descriptor model (or generated mapping) where key, parser, patch target, and apply target are defined once and consumed by parser/apply paths; enforce completeness with compile-time or table-driven tests.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::t.summary: Transition handling is implemented with repeated per-event/per-action boilerplate across runtime handlers instead of a unified declarative rule engine.

evidence: `rectify.go` (607 lines) defines several large reducer maps and repetitive glue handlers for different transition families.
`urbit_operations_runtime.go` (549 lines) repeats `runUrbitTransitionTemplateFn` scaffolding with similar lifecycle parameters and localized action logic across many operations.
`leak/handler.go` adds another custom action-routing pipeline with manual payload-type switching and auth routing, reinforcing repeated bespoke transition/action dispatch patterns.
detail.suggestion: Create a shared declarative transition/action registry with reusable step pipelines (validation, state mutation, side-effects, broadcast) so new actions are data entries plus focused handlers instead of repeated orchestration scaffolding.

review::.::holistic::design_coherence::s.summary: Session/logstream behavior is split between global state functions and a pass-through runtime interface, creating an incoherent ownership model.

evidence: `session.go` defines global mutable session state plus `LogstreamRuntime` and `logstreamRuntime` methods that mostly forward to package-level functions.
The runtime abstraction only owns one channel while most behaviors are still delegated to global functions, so abstraction boundaries do not map cleanly to owned state.
`logger.go` depends on this sink/runtime path (`ConfigureLogstreamRuntime`), binding logging flow to mixed global/runtime session ownership.
detail.suggestion: Consolidate session and logstream state into a single manager type implementing a narrow interface, then inject that manager where needed (logger/handlers) and retire pass-through wrappers over package globals.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Startup treats version discovery as successful on any channel receive, even explicit failure signals.

evidence: `startVersionDiscovery` sends `versionUpdate` from `config.SyncVersionInfo()` into `versionUpdateChannel`.
`waitForVersionDiscovery` uses `case <-versionUpdate:` and unconditionally logs "Version info retrieved"; it does not read/use the bool.
`SyncVersionInfo` returns `false` on fetch/persist failures, so startup can report success and skip fallback despite failed discovery.
detail.suggestion: Change `waitForVersionDiscovery` to read the bool (`ok := <-versionUpdate`) and treat `false` as failure: log a failure message, load local version fallback, and optionally return/propagate an error so startup health reflects degraded state.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Background services use incompatible failure contracts, causing updater failures to be logged-and-dropped while peers fail fast.

evidence: `superviseBackgroundService` expects service functions to return errors for health/reporting.
In `routines/version.go`, `callUpdater`/`updateBinary` handle many operational failures by logging and `return` (void), so `CheckVersionLoopWithContext` typically never returns these failures.
By contrast, `OldLogsCleanerWithContext` escalates repeated failures by returning an error after threshold, so supervised behavior differs materially across services.
detail.suggestion: Standardize background loop contracts: return structured errors from updater paths (or an error channel) and let the supervisor decide retry/backoff/escalation. Keep logging, but do not make logging the terminal error sink for recoverable/retriable operational failures.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: WebSocket write failures in session broadcast are downgraded to state flips without error context or consistent cleanup.

evidence: `BroadcastAuth`/`BroadcastUnauth` call `client.Write`, then only set `client.Active = false` on error and continue; no log, close, or explicit removal path is triggered there.
`SysLogStreamerWithContext` handles write errors with explicit log + `conn.Close()` + removal scheduling, preserving failure context and cleanup intent.
Leak path (`reportLeakError`) logs queueing failures, showing another different write-failure policy for socket/channel boundaries.
detail.suggestion: Introduce a shared connection-write error policy for websocket boundaries: always attach context (client/token/channel), close the connection, schedule removal, and emit one structured error signal/log path.
